It's assigned to one of the executors for processing.\n    50→ * 2. It's locked for committing, removal, other manipulations etc.\n    51→ * 3. Neither 1 or 2, i.e. it stays idle. This is possible if we do not have enough executors or because those tasks\n    52→ *    are not processable (e.g. because no records fetched) yet.\n    53→ */\n    54→public final class DefaultTaskManager implements TaskManager {\n    55→\n    56→    private final Time time;\n    57→    private final Logger log;\n    58→    private final TasksRegistry tasks;\n    59→\n    60→    private final Lock tasksLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    61→    private final Condition tasksCondition = tasksLock.newCondition();\n    62→    private final List<TaskId> lockedTasks = new ArrayList<>();\n    63→    private final Map<TaskId, StreamsException> uncaughtExceptions = new HashMap<>();\n    64→    private final Map<TaskId, TaskExecutor> assignedTasks = new HashMap<>();\n    65→    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n    66→\n    67→    private final List<TaskExecutor> taskExecutors;\n    68→\n    69→    public static class DefaultTaskExecutorCreator implements TaskExecutorCreator {\n    70→        @Override\n    71→        public TaskExecutor create(final TaskManager taskManager, final String name, final Time time, final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata) {\n    72→            return new DefaultTaskExecutor(taskManager, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata);\n    73→        }\n    74→    }\n    75→\n    76→    public DefaultTaskManager(final Time time,\n    77→                              final String clientId,\n    78→                              final TasksRegistry tasks,\n    79→                              final TaskExecutorCreator executorCreator,\n    80→                              final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata,\n    81→                              final int numExecutors\n    82→                              ) {\n    83→        final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", clientId);\n    84→        final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n    85→        this.log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskManager.class);\n    86→        this.time = time;\n    87→        this.tasks = tasks;\n    88→        this.taskExecutionMetadata = taskExecutionMetadata;\n    89→\n    90→        this.taskExecutors = new ArrayList<>(numExecutors);\n    91→        for (int i = 1; i <= numExecutors; i++) {\n    92→            final String name = clientId + \"-TaskExecutor-\" + i;\n    93→            this.taskExecutors.add(executorCreator.create(this, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata));\n    94→        }\n    95→    }\n    96→\n    97→    @Override\n    98→    public StreamTask assignNextTask(final TaskExecutor executor) {\n    99→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   100→            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n   101→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for getting next task to assign is unrecognized\");\n   102→            }\n   103→\n   104→            // the most naive scheduling algorithm for now: give the next unlocked, unassigned, and  processable task\n   105→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   106→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   107→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   108→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   109→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   110→                ) {\n   111→\n   112→                    assignedTasks.put(task.id(), executor);\n   113→\n   114→                    log.debug(\"Assigned task {} to executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n   115→\n   116→                    return task;\n   117→                }\n   118→            }\n   119→\n   120→            log.debug(\"Found no assignable task for executor {}\", executor.name());\n   121→\n   122→            return null;\n   123→        });\n   124→    }\n   125→\n   126→    @Override\n   127→    public void awaitProcessableTasks(final Supplier<Boolean> isShuttingDown) throws InterruptedException {\n   128→        final boolean interrupted = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   129→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   130→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   131→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   132→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   133→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   134→                ) {\n   135→                    log.debug(\"Await unblocked: returning early from await since a processable task {} was found\", task.id());\n   136→                    return false;\n   137→                }\n   138→            }\n   139→            try {\n   140→                // We re-check the shutdownRequest atomic boolean to avoid a race condition. If this thread was\n   141→                // previously interrupted while awaiting tasksCondition, it is possible to miss the signalAll that\n   142→                // is called during shutdown. If this happens, we end up blocking in the await forever.\n   143→                if (!isShuttingDown.get()) {\n   144→                    log.debug(\"Await blocking\");\n   145→                    tasksCondition.await();\n   146→                } else {\n   147→                    log.debug(\"Not awaiting since shutdown was requested\");\n   148→                }\n   149→            } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n   150→                // we interrupt the thread for shut down and pause.\n   151→                // we can ignore this exception.\n   152→                log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Interrupted while waiting for processable tasks\");\n   153→                return true;\n   154→            }\n   155→            log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Woken up to check for processable tasks\");\n   156→            return false;\n   157→        });\n   158→\n   159→        if (interrupted) {\n   160→            throw new InterruptedException();\n   161→        }\n   162→    }\n   163→\n   164→    public void signalTaskExecutors() {\n   165→        log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   166→        executeWithTasksLocked(tasksCondition::signalAll);\n   167→    }\n   168→\n   169→    @Override\n   170→    public void unassignTask(final StreamTask task, final TaskExecutor executor) {\n   171→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   172→            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n   173→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for unassign task is unrecognized\");\n   174→            }\n   175→\n   176→            final TaskExecutor lockedExecutor = assignedTasks.get(task.id());\n   177→            if (lockedExecutor == null || lockedExecutor != executor) {\n   178→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Task \" + task.id() + \" is not locked by the executor\");\n   179→            }\n   180→\n   181→            assignedTasks.remove(task.id());\n   182→\n   183→            log.debug(\"Unassigned {} from executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n   184→            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n   185→        });\n   186→    }\n   187→\n   188→    @Override\n   189→    public KafkaFuture<Void> lockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n   190→        final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> result = new KafkaFutureImpl<>();\n   191→\n   192→        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   193→            result.complete(null);\n   194→            return result;\n   195→        }\n   196→\n   197→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   198→            lockedTasks.addAll(taskIds);\n   199→\n   200→            final Set<TaskId> remainingTaskIds = new ConcurrentSkipListSet<>(taskIds);\n   201→\n   202→            for (final TaskId taskId : taskIds) {\n   203→                final Task task = tasks.initializedTask(taskId);\n   204→\n   205→                if (task == null) {\n   206→                    throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Trying to lock task \" + taskId + \" but it's not owned\");\n   207→                }\n   208→\n   209→                if (!task.isActive()) {\n   210→                    throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The locking task \" + taskId + \" is not an active task\");\n   211→                }\n   212→\n   213→                if (assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   214→                    final TaskExecutor executor = assignedTasks.get(taskId);\n   215→                    log.debug(\"Requesting release of task {} from {}\", taskId, executor.name());\n   216→                    final KafkaFuture<StreamTask> future = executor.unassign();\n   217→                    future.whenComplete((streamTask, throwable) -> {\n   218→                        if (throwable != null) {\n   219→                            result.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n   220→                        } else {\n   221→                            assert !assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId);\n   222→                            // It can happen that the executor handed back the task before we asked it to\n   223→                            // in which case `streamTask` will be null here.\n   224→                            assert streamTask == null || streamTask.id() == taskId;\n   225→                            remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n   226→                            if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   227→                                result.complete(null);\n   228→                            }\n   229→                        }\n   230→                    });\n   231→                } else {\n   232→                    remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n   233→                    if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   234→                        result.complete(null);\n   235→                    }\n   236→                }\n   237→            }\n   238→\n   239→            return result;\n   240→        });\n   241→    }\n   242→\n   243→    @Override\n   244→    public KafkaFuture<Void> lockAllTasks() {\n   245→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() ->\n   246→            lockTasks(tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(Task::id).collect(Collectors.toSet()))\n   247→        );\n   248→    }\n   249→\n   250→    @Override\n   251→    public void unlockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n   252→\n   253→        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   254→            return;\n   255→        }\n   256→\n   257→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   258→            lockedTasks.removeAll(taskIds);\n   259→            log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   260→            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n   261→        });\n   262→    }\n   263→\n   264→    @Override\n   265→    public void unlockAllTasks() {\n   266→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> unlockTasks(tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(Task::id).collect(Collectors.toSet())));\n   267→    }\n   268→\n   269→    @Override\n   270→    public void add(final Set<StreamTask> tasksToAdd) {\n   271→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   272→            for (final StreamTask task : tasksToAdd) {\n   273→                tasks.addActiveTask(task);\n   274→            }\n   275→            log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   276→            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n   277→        });\n   278→\n   279→        log.info(\"Added tasks {} to the task manager to process\", tasksToAdd);\n   280→    }\n   281→\n   282→    @Override\n   283→    public void remove(final TaskId taskId) {\n   284→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   285→            if (assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   286→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is still assigned to executors\");\n   287→            }\n   288→\n   289→            if (!lockedTasks.contains(taskId)) {\n   290→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is not locked yet by the task manager\");\n   291→            }\n   292→\n   293→            if (!tasks.containsInitialized(taskId)) {\n   294→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is not owned by the task manager\");\n   295→            }\n   296→\n   297→            tasks.removeTask(tasks.initializedTask(taskId));\n   298→        });\n   299→\n   300→        log.info(\"Removed task {} from the task manager\", taskId);\n   301→    }\n   302→\n   303→    @Override\n   304→    public Set<ReadOnlyTask> getTasks() {\n   305→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(ReadOnlyTask::new).collect(Collectors.toSet()));\n   306→    }\n   307→\n   308→    @Override\n   309→    public void setUncaughtException(final StreamsException exception, final TaskId taskId) {\n   310→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   311→\n   312→            if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   313→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"An uncaught exception can only be set as long as the task is still assigned\");\n   314→            }\n   315→\n   316→            if (uncaughtExceptions.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   317→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The uncaught exception must be cleared before restarting processing\");\n   318→            }\n   319→\n   320→            uncaughtExceptions.put(taskId, exception);\n   321→        });\n   322→\n   323→        log.info(\"Set an uncaught exception of type {} for task {}, with error message: {}\",\n   324→            exception.getClass().getName(),\n   325→            taskId,\n   326→            exception.getMessage());\n   327→    }\n   328→\n   329→    public Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> drainUncaughtExceptions() {\n   330→        final Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> returnValue = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   331→            final Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> result = new HashMap<>(uncaughtExceptions);\n   332→            uncaughtExceptions.clear();\n   333→            return result;\n   334→        });\n   335→\n   336→        if (!returnValue.isEmpty()) {\n   337→            log.debug(\"Drained {} uncaught exceptions\", returnValue.size());\n   338→        }\n   339→\n   340→        return returnValue;\n   341→    }\n   342→\n   343→    public boolean hasUncaughtException(final TaskId taskId) {\n   344→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> uncaughtExceptions.containsKey(taskId));\n   345→    }\n   346→\n   347→    private void executeWithTasksLocked(final Runnable action) {\n   348→        tasksLock.lock();\n   349→        try {\n   350→            action.run();\n   351→        } finally {\n   352→            tasksLock.unlock();\n   353→        }\n   354→    }\n   355→\n   356→    private <T> T returnWithTasksLocked(final Supplier<T> action) {\n   357→        tasksLock.lock();\n   358→        try {\n   359→            return action.get();\n   360→        } finally {\n   361→            tasksLock.unlock();\n   362→        }\n   363→    }\n   364→\n   365→    private boolean canProgress(final StreamTask task, final long nowMs) {\n   366→        return\n   367→            taskExecutionMetadata.canProcessTask(task, nowMs) && task.isProcessable(nowMs) ||\n   368→                taskExecutionMetadata.canPunctuateTask(task) && (task.canPunctuateStreamTime() || task.canPunctuateSystemTime());\n   369→    }\n   370→\n   371→    public void startTaskExecutors() {\n   372→        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n   373→            t.start();\n   374→        }\n   375→    }\n   376→\n   377→    public void shutdown(final Duration duration) {\n   378→        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n   379→            t.requestShutdown();\n   380→        }\n   381→        signalTaskExecutors();\n   382→        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n     version.topologyLock.unlock();\n   187→        }\n   188→    }\n   189→\n   190→    public void maybeNotifyTopologyVersionListeners() {\n   191→        try {\n   192→            lock();\n   193→            final long minThreadVersion = minimumThreadVersion();\n   194→            final Iterator<TopologyVersionListener> iterator = version.activeTopologyUpdateListeners.listIterator();\n   195→            TopologyVersionListener topologyVersionListener;\n   196→            while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n   197→                topologyVersionListener = iterator.next();\n   198→ topologyVersionListener.future.complete(null);\n   201→                    iterator.remove();\n   202→                    log.info(\"All threads are now on topology version {}\", topologyVersionListener.topologyVersion);\n   203→                }\n   204→            }\n   205→        } finally {\n   206→            unlock();\n   207→        }\n   208→    }\n   209→\n   210→    // Return the minimum version across all live threads, or Long.MAX_VALUE if there are no threads running\n   211→    private long minimumThreadVersion() {\n   212→        final Optional<Long> minVersion = threadVersions.values().stream().min(Long::compare);\n   213→        return minVersion.orElse(Long.MAX_VALUE);\n   214→    }\n   215→\n   216→    public void wakeupThreads() {\n   217→        try {\n   218→            lock();\n   219→            version.topologyCV.signalAll();\n   220→        } finally {\n   221→            unlock();\n   222→        }\n   223→    }\n   224→\n   225→    public void maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology(final Supplier<StreamThread.State> threadState) {\n   226→        if (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n   227→            try {\n   228→                lock();\n   229→                while (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n   230→                    try {\n   231→                        log.debug(\"Detected that the topology is               log.error(\"StreamThread was interrupted while waiting on empty topology\", e);\n   235→                    }\n   236→                }\n   237→            } finally {\n   238→                unlock();\n   239→            }\n   240→        }\n   241→    }\n   242→\n   243→    /**\n   244→     * Adds the topology and registers a future that listens for all threads on the older version to see the update\n   245→     */\n   246→    public void registerAndBuildNewTopology(final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> future, final InternalTopologyBuilder newTopologyBuilder) {\n   247→        try {\n   248→            lock();\n   249→            buildAndVerifyTopology(newTopologyBuilder);\n   250→            log.info(\"New NamedTopology {} passed validation and will be added, old topology version is {}\", newTopologyBuilder.topologyName(), version.topologyVersion.get());\n   251→            version.topologyVersion.incrementAndGet();\n   252→ 270→    }\n   271→\n   272→    /**\n   273→     * Checks if a given topology is paused.\n   274→     * @param topologyName If null, assume that we are checking the `UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY`.\n   275→     * 290→     * Removes the topology and registers a future that listens for all threads on the older version to see the update\n   291→     */\n   292→    public KafkaFuture<Void> unregisterTopology(final removedBuilder.allSourcePatternStrings().forEach(allInputTopics::remove);\n   302→            log.info(\"Finished removing NamedTopology {}, topology version was updated to {}\", topologyName,     removeTopologyFuture.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n   306→        } finally {\n   307→            unlock();\n   308→        }\n   309→        return removeTopologyFuture;\n   310→    }\n   311→\n   312→    public TaskConfig taskConfig(final TaskId taskId) {\n   313→        final InternalTopologyBuilder builder = lookupBuilderForTask(taskId);\n   314→        return builder.topologyConfigs().getTaskConfig();\n   315→    }\n   316→\n   317→    public void buildAndRewriteTopology() {\n   318→        applyToEachBuilder(this::buildAndVerifyTopology);\n   319→    }\n   320→\n   321→    private void buildAndVerifyTopology(final InternalTopologyBuilder builder) {\n   322→        builder.rewriteTopology(config);\n   323→        builder.buildTopology();\n   324→\n   325→        final Set<String> allInputTopicsCopy = new HashSet<>(allInputTopics);\n   326→\n   327→        // As we go, check each topology for overlap in the set of input topics/patterns\n   328→        final int numInputTopics = allInputTopicsCopy.size();\n   329→        final List<String> inputTopics = builder.fullSourceTopicNames();\n   330→        final Collection<String> inputPatterns = builder.allSourcePatternStrings();\n   331→\n   332→        final Set<String> newInputTopics = new HashSet<>(inputTopics);\n   333→        newInputTopics.addAll(inputPatterns);\n   334→\n   335→        final int numNewInputTopics = newInputTopics.size();\n   336→        allInputTopicsCopy.addAll(newInputTopics);\n   337→\n   338→        if (allInputTopicsCopy.size() != numInputTopics + numNewInputTopics) {\n   339→            inputTopics.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n   340→            inputPatterns.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n   341→            log.error(\"Tried to add the NamedTopology {} but it had overlap with other input topics {} or patterns {}\",\n   342→                      builder.topologyName(), inputTopics, inputPatterns);\n   343→            throw new TopologyException(\"Named Topologies may not subscribe to the same input topics or patterns\");\n   344→        }\n   345→\n   346→        final ProcessorTopology globalTopology = builder.buildGlobalStateTopology();\n   347→        if (globalTopology != null) {\n   348→            if (builder.topologyName() != null) {\n   349→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Global state stores are not supported with Named Topologies\");\n   350→            } else if (this.globalTopology != null) {\n   351→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology builder had global state, but global topology has already been set\");\n   352→            } else {\n   353→                this.globalTopology = globalTopology;\n   354→                globalStateStores.putAll(builder.globalStateStores());\n   355→            }\n   356→        }\n   357→        allInputTopics.addAll(newInputTopics);\n   358→    }\n   359→\n   360→    public int numStreamThreads(final StreamsConfig config) {\n   361→        final int configuredNumStreamThreads = config.getInt(StreamsConfig.NUM_STREAM_THREADS_CONFIG);\n   362→\n   363→        // If there are named topologies but some are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n   364→        if (hasNamedTopologies()) {\n   365→            if (hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n   366→                log.error(\"Detected a named topology with no input topics, a named topology may not be empty.\");\n   367→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n   368→                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or pattern.\");\n   369→            }\n   370→        } else {\n   371→            // If both the global and non-global topologies are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n   372→            if (hasNoLocalTopology() && !hasGlobalTopology()) {\n   373→                log.error(\"Topology with no input topics will create no stream threads and no global thread.\");\n   374→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n   375→                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or global table.\");\n   376→            }\n   377→        }\n   378→\n   379→        // Lastly we check for an empty non-global topology and override the threads to zero if set otherwise\n   380→        if (configuredNumStreamThreads != 0 && hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n   381→            log.info(\"Overriding number of StreamThreads to zero for global-only topology\");\n   382→            return 0;\n   383→        }\n   384→\n   385→        return configuredNumStreamThreads;\n   386→    }\n   387→\n   388→    /**\n   389→     * @return true iff the app is using named topologies, or was started up with no topology at all\n   390→     */\n   391→    public boolean || evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasOffsetResetOverrides);\n   429→    }\n   430→\n   431→    public Optional<AutoOffsetResetStrategy> offsetResetStrategy(final String topic) {\n   432→        for (final InternalTopologyBuilder builder : builders.values()) {\n   433→            if (builder.containsTopic(topic)) {\n   434→                return Optional.ofNullable(builder.offsetResetStrategy(topic));\n   435→            }\n   436→        }\n   437→        log.warn(\"Unable to look up offset reset strategy for topic {} \" +\n   438→ not \" +\n   440→                \"persist or appear frequently.\",\n   441→            topic, namedTopologiesView()\n   442→        );\n   443→        // returning `null` for an Optional return type return null;\n   447→    }\n   448→\n   449→\n   450→    public Collection<String> fullSourceTopicNamesForTopology(final String topologyName) {\n   451→        Objects.requireNonNull(topologyName, one or more input topics and\n   122→ * sends output to zero, one, or more output topics.\n   123→ * <p>\n   124→ * The computational logic can be specified either by using the {@link Topology} to define a DAG topology of\n   125→ * {@link org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.api.Processor}s or by using the {@link StreamsBuilder} which provides the high-level DSL to define\n   126→ * transformations.\n   127→ * <p>\n   128→ * One {@code KafkaStreams} instance can contain one or more threads specified in the configs for the processing work.\n   129→ * <p>\n   130→ * A {@code KafkaStreams} instance can co-ordinate with any other instances with the same\n   131→ * {@link StreamsConfig#APPLICATION_ID_CONFIG application ID} (whether in the same process, on other processes on this\n   132→ * machine, or on remote machines) as a single (possibly distributed) stream processing application.\n   133→ * These instances will divide up the work based on the assignment of the input topic partitions so that all partitions\n   134→ * are being consumed.\n   135→ * If instances are added or fail, all (remaining) instances will rebalance the partition assignment among themselves\n   136→ * to balance processing load and ensure that all input topic partitions are processed.\n   137→ * <p>\n   138→ * Internally a {@code KafkaStreams} instance contains a normal {@link KafkaProducer} and {@link KafkaConsumer} instance\n   139→ * that is used for reading input and writing output.\n   140→ * <p>\n   141→ * A simple example might look like this:\n   142→ *  220→            final byte[] rawOldValue = rawNewValue == null || sendOldValues ? wrapped().get(entry.key()) : null;\n   221→\n   222→            // this is an optimization: if this key did not exist Now I have enough information to write a comprehensive report. Let me create the final summary document:\n\n## Kafka Streams Deadlock Analysis Report\n\nBased on my thorough exploration of the Kafka Streams source code at `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/`, I have identified multiple potential deadlock scenarios. Here are my findings:\n\n### CRITICAL DEADLOCK SCENARIOS\n\n---\n\n### **1. NESTED SYNCHRONIZED BLOCK DEADLOCK in CachingKeyValueStore**\n\n**Location:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/state/internals/CachingKeyValueStore.java`\n\n**Lines:** 118-122, 146-166, 277-295\n\n**Issue:** Multiple nested monitor locks with potential lock ordering violation\n\n```\nMethod: getPosition() [lines 114-125]\n  synchronized (position) {\n    synchronized (wrappedPosition) {\n      // Lock ordering: position -> wrappedPosition\n    }\n  }\n\nMethod: query() [lines 146-166]\n  readLock.lock()  // ReadWriteLock\n  try {\n    getPosition()  // Acquires synchronized locks above\n  }\n\nMethod: putInternal() [lines 275-296]\n  synchronized (position) {\n    // Called from put(), putIfAbsent(), putAll()\n    // which hold writeLock\n  }\n```\n\n**Deadlock Scenario:**\n- Thread A: Acquires `readLock` via `query()`, then calls `getPosition()` trying to acquire `position` lock\n- Thread B: Acquires `writeLock` via `put()`, acquires `position` lock, then tries to modify state\n- If Thread B needs to wait for `readLock` (for other operations), circular wait occurs\n\n**Root Cause:** Nested lock acquisition (ReadWriteLock -> synchronized objects) without consistent ordering.\n\n---\n\n### **2. MULTIPLE SEQUENTIAL LOCK ACQUISITION in DefaultStateUpdater**\n\n**Location:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/DefaultStateUpdater.java`\n\n**Lines:** 1092-1103\n\n**Issue:** Method acquires three ReentrantLocks in sequence\n\n```java\nprivate <T> Set<T> executeWithQueuesLocked(final Supplier<Set<T>> action) {\n    tasksAndActionsLock.lock();           // Lock 1\n    restoredActiveTasksLock.lock();       // Lock 2\n    exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock.lock();  // Lock 3\n    try {\n        return action.get();\n    } finally {\n        exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock.unlock();\n        restoredActiveTasksLock.unlock();\n        tasksAndActionsLock.unlock();\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Called from:**\n- `tasks()` [line 1052]\n- `restoresActiveTasks()` [line 1057]\n- `activeTasks()` [line 1063]\n- `standbyTasks()` [line 1070]\n\n**Deadlock Potential:**\n- Other methods in the StateUpdaterThread acquire these locks in different orders:\n  - `performActionsOnTasks()` [line 218]: acquires `tasksAndActionsLock`\n  - `addToExceptionsAndFailedTasksThenRemoveFromUpdatingTasks()` [line 419]: acquires `exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock`\n  - `addToRestoredTasks()` [line 708]: acquires `restoredActiveTasksLock`\n\nIf a callback or condition signal happens while holding one lock and waiting for another, deadlock occurs.\n\n---\n\n### **3. LOCK HELD DURING EXTERNAL CALLBACK INVOCATION - StreamThread**\n\n**Location:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/StreamThread.java`\n\n**Lines:** 253-291\n\n**Critical Pattern:**\n\n```java\nState setState(final State newState) {\n    final State oldState;\n    synchronized (stateLock) {  // Acquire stateLock\n        // ... state change logic ...\n        stateLock.notifyAll();\n    }  // Release stateLock\n    \n    // OUTSIDE the lock, but...\n    if (stateListener != null) {\n        stateListener.onChange(this, state, oldState);  // User callback!\n    }\n    return oldState;\n}\n```\n\n**However, also see:** Line 280 which DOES call `updateThreadMetadata()` INSIDE the lock:\n```java\nif (newState == State.RUNNING) {\n    updateThreadMetadata(taskManager.activeTaskMap(), taskManager.standbyTaskMap());\n}\n```\n\nThis calls `taskManager.activeTaskMap()` which internally calls `Tasks` synchronized methods while holding `stateLock`.\n\n**Deadlock Scenario:**\n- Thread A (StreamThread): Holds `stateLock`, calls `updateThreadMetadata()`, which calls `taskManager.activeTaskMap()`\n- Thread B (TaskManager/Main thread): Tries to acquire `stateLock` from `KafkaStreams.metadataForLocalThreads()` [line 1979], but needs task information\n- If Thread B is blocked waiting for task synchronization while Thread A waits for it, deadlock occurs\n\n---\n\n### **4. NESTED SYNCHRONIZED BLOCKS - ThreadCache and NamedCache**\n\n**Location:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/state/internals/ThreadCache.java`\n\n**Lines:** 80-100\n\n**Issue:**\n\n```java\npublic synchronized void resize(final long newCacheSizeBytes) {  // Lock: ThreadCache\n    while (sizeInBytes.get() > maxCacheSizeBytes) {\n        final NamedCache cache = circularIterator.next();\n        synchronized (cache) {  // Nested lock: NamedCache\n            final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n            cache.evict();\n            sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(cache.sizeInBytes() - oldSize);\n        }\n        numEvicts++;\n    }\n}\n```\n\nAlso called during flush operations [lines 138-150] with similar nesting.\n\n**Deadlock Risk:** If `cache` operations trigger events or callbacks that try to access the outer ThreadCache, circular dependency.\n\n---\n\n### **5. LOCK HELD ACROSS CONDITION WAIT - DefaultTaskManager**\n\n**Location:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/tasks/DefaultTaskManager.java`\n\n**Lines:** 127-162, 164-167\n\n**Issue:**\n\n```java\npublic void awaitProcessableTasks(final Supplier<Boolean> isShuttingDown) throws InterruptedException {\n    final boolean interrupted = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n        // ... checking logic ...\n        try {\n            if (!isShuttingDown.get()) {\n                tasksCondition.await();  // Waits on condition while holding tasksLock\n            }\n        } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n            return true;\n        }\n        return false;\n    });\n}\n\npublic void signalTaskExecutors() {\n    executeWithTasksLocked(tasksCondition::signalAll);  // Must hold lock to signal\n}\n\n@Override\npublic void unassignTask(final StreamTask task, final TaskExecutor executor) {\n    executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n        // ... logic ...\n        tasksCondition.signalAll();  // Called while holding tasksLock\n    });\n}\n```\n\n**Deadlock Potential:** While `tasksCondition.await()` releases the lock, if the condition is signaled and immediately re-acquired before the waiting thread can progress, and if there are other threads waiting for the same lock in a different state, deadlock can occur.\n\n---\n\n### **6. CachingKeyValueStore\n    56→    extends WrappedStateStore<KeyValueStore<Bytes, byte[]>, byte[], byte[]>\n    57→    implements KeyValueStore<Bytes, byte[]>, CachedStateStore<byte[], byte[]> {\n    58→\n    59→    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(CachingKeyValueStore.class);\n    60→\n    61→    private CacheFlushListener<byte[], byte[]> flushListener;\n    62→   public interface CacheQueryHandler {\n    72→        QueryResult<?> apply(\n    73→            final Query<?> query,\n    74→            final Position mergedPosition,\n    75→            final PositionBound positionBound,\n    76→            final QueryConfig config,\n    77→            final StateStore store\n    78→        );\n    79→    }\n    80→\n    81→    @SuppressWarnings(\"rawtypes\")\n    82→    private final Map<Class, CacheQueryHandler> queryHandlers =\n    83→        mkMap(\n    84→            mkEntry(\n    85→                KeyQuery.class,\n    86→                (query, mergedPosition, positionBound, config, store) ->\n    87→                    runKeyQuery(query, mergedPosition, positionBound, config)\n    88→            )\n    89→        );\n    90→\n    91→\n    92→    CachingKeyValueStore(final KeyValueStore<Bytes, byte[]> underlying, final CacheType cacheType) {\n    93→        super(underlying);\n    94→        position = StateStore root) {\n   100→        internalContext = asInternalProcessorContext(stateStoreContext);\n   101→        cacheName = ThreadCache.nameSpaceFromTaskIdAndStore(internalContext.taskId().toString(), name());\n   102→        internalContext.registerCacheFlushListener(cacheName, entries -> {\n   103→            for (final ThreadCache.DirtyEntry entry : entries) {\n   104→                putAndMaybeForward(entry, internalContext);\n   105→            }\n   106→        });\n   107→        super.init(stateStoreContext, position as well\n   116→        final Position mergedPosition = Position.emptyPosition();\n   117→        final Position wrappedPosition = wrapped().getPosition();\n   118→        synchronized (position) {\n   119→            synchronized (wrappedPosition) {\n   120→                mergedPosition.merge(position);\n   121→                mergedPosition.merge(wrappedPosition);\n   122→            }\n   123→        }\n   124→        return mergedPosition;\n   125→    }\n   126→\n   127→    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   128→    @Override\n   129→    public <R> QueryResult<R> query(final Query<R> query,\n   130→                                    final PositionBound positionBound,\n   131→                                    final QueryConfig config) {\n   132→\n   133→        if (cacheType == CacheType.TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE_WITH_HEADERS) {\n   134→            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\"Queries (IQv2) are not supported for timestamped key-value stores with headers yet.\");\n   135→        }\n   136→\n   137→        final long start = config.isCollectExecutionInfo() ? System.nanoTime() : -1L;\n   138→        final QueryResult<R> result;\n   139→\n   140→        final CacheQueryHandler handler = queryHandlers.get(query.getClass());\n   141→        if (handler == null) {\n   142→            result = wrapped().query(query, positionBound, config);\n   143→\n   144→        } else {\n   145→            final int partition = internalContext.taskId().partition();\n   146→            final Lock lock = this.lock.readLock();\n   147→            lock.lock();\n   148→            try {\n   149→                validateStoreOpen();\n   150→                final Position mergedPosition = getPosition();\n   151→\n   152→                // We use the merged position since the cache and the store may be at different positions\n   153→                if (!StoreQueryUtils.isPermitted(mergedPosition, positionBound, partition)) {\n   154→                    result = QueryResult.notUpToBound(mergedPosition, positionBound, partition);\n   155→                } else {\n   156→                    result = (QueryResult<R>) handler.apply(\n   157→                        query,\n   158→                        mergedPosition,\n   159→                        positionBound,\n   160→                        config,\n   161→                        this\n   162→                    );\n   163→                }\n   164→            } finally {\n   165→                lock.unlock();\n   166→            }\n   167→        }\n   168→        if (config.isCollectExecutionInfo()) {\n   169→            result.addExecutionInfo(\n   170→                \"Handled in \" + getClass() + \" in \" + (System.nanoTime() - start) + \"ns\");\n   171→        }\n   172→        return result;\n   173→    }\n   174→\n   175→    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   176→    private <R> QueryResult<R> runKeyQuery(final Query<R> query,\n   177→                                           final Position mergedPosition,\n   178→                                           final PositionBound positionBound,\n   179→                                           final QueryConfig config) {\n   180→        if (cacheType == CacheType.TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE_WITH_HEADERS) {\n   181→            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\"Queries (IQv2) are not supported for timestamped key-value stores with headers yet.\");\n   182→        }\n   183→        QueryResult<R> result = null;\n   184→        final KeyQuery<Bytes, byte[]> keyQuery = (KeyQuery<Bytes, byte[]>) query;\n   185→\n   186→        if (keyQuery.isSkipCache()) {\n   187→            return wrapped().query(query, positionBound, config);\n   188→        }\n   189→\n   190→        final Bytes key = keyQuery.getKey();\n   191→\n   192→        synchronized (mergedPosition) {\n   193→            if (internalContext.cache() != null) {\n   194→                final LRUCacheEntry lruCacheEntry = internalContext.cache().get(cacheName, key);\n   195→                if (lruCacheEntry != null) {\n   196→                    final byte[] rawValue;\n   197→                    if (cacheType == CacheType.TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE && !WrappedStateStore.isTimestamped(wrapped()) && !StoreQueryUtils.isAdapter(wrapped())) {\n   198→                        rawValue = ValueAndTimestampDeserializer.rawValue(lruCacheEntry.value());\n   199→                    } else {\n   200→                        rawValue = lruCacheEntry.value();\n   201→                    }\n   202→                    result = (QueryResult<R>) QueryResult.forResult(rawValue);\n   203→                }\n   204→            }\n   205→\n   206→            // We don't need to check the position at the state store since we already performed the check on\n   207→            // the merged position above\n   208→            if (result == null) {\n   209→                result = wrapped().query(query, PositionBound.unbounded(), config);\n   210→            }\n   211→            result.setPosition(mergedPosition.copy());\n   212→        }\n   213→        return result;\n   214→    }\n   215→\n   216→    private void putAndMaybeForward(final ThreadCache.DirtyEntry entry,\n   217→                                    final InternalProcessorContext<?, ?> context) {\n   218→        if (flushListener != null) {\n   219→            final byte[] rawNewValue = entry.newValue();\n   220→            final byte[] rawOldValue = rawNewValue == null || sendOldValues ? wrapped().get(entry.key()) : null;\n   221→\n   222→            // this is an optimization: if this key did not exist in underlying store and also not in the cache,\n   223→            // we can skip flushing to downstream as well as writing to underlying store\n   224→            if (rawNewValue != null || rawOldValue != null) {\n   225→                // we need to get the old values if needed, and then put to store, and then flush\n   226→                final ProcessorRecordContext current = context.recordContext();\n   227→                try {\n   228→                    context.setRecordContext(entry.entry().context());\n   229→                    wrapped().put(entry.key(), entry.newValue());\n   230→                    flushListener.apply(\n   231→                        new Record<>(\n   232→                            entry.key().get(),\n   233→                            new Change<>(rawNewValue, sendOldValues ? rawOldValue : null),\n   234→                            entry.entry().context().timestamp(),\n   235→                            entry.entry().context().headers()));\n   236→                } finally {\n   237→                    context.setRecordContext(current);\n   238→                }\n   239→            }\n   240→        } else {\n   241→            final ProcessorRecordContext current = context.recordContext();\n   242→            try {\n   243→                context.setRecordContext(entry.entry().context());\n   244→                wrapped().put(entry.key(), entry.newValue());\n   245→            } finally {\n   246→                context.setRecordContext(current);\n   247→            }\n   248→        }\n   249→    }\n   250→\n   251→    @Override\n   252→    public boolean setFlushListener(final CacheFlushListener<byte[], byte[]> flushListener,\n   253→                                    final boolean @Override\n   261→    public void put(final Bytes key,\n   262→                    final byte[] value) {\n   263→        Objects.requireNonNull(key, \"key cannot be null\");\n   264→ 300→                              final byte[] value) {\n   301→        Objects.requireNonNull(key, \"key cannot be null\");\n   302→        validateStoreOpen();\n   303→ org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.ProcessorRecordContext;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.query.KeyQuery;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.query.Position;\nimport java.util.concurrent.locks.ReadWriteLock;\nimport java.util.concurrent.locks.ReentrantReadWriteLock;\n\nimport static org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils.mkEntry;\nimport static org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils.mkMap;\nimport static org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.ProcessorContextUtils.asInternalProcessorContext;\nimport static org.apache.kafka.streams.state.internals.ExceptionUtils.executeAll;\nimport static org.apache.kafka.streams.state.internals.ExceptionUtils.throwSuppressed;\n\npublic class CachingKeyValueStore\n    extends WrappedStateStore<KeyValueStore<Bytes, byte[]>, byte[], byte[]>\n    implements KeyValueStore<Bytes, byte[]>, CachedStateStore<byte[], byte[]> {\n\n    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(CachingKeyValueStore.class);\n\n    private CacheFlushListener<byte[], byte[]> flushListener;\n    private boolean sendOldValues;\n    private String cacheName;\n    private InternalProcessorContext<?, ?> internalContext;\n    private Thread streamThread;\n    private final ReadWriteLock lock = new ReentrantReadWriteLock();\n    private final Position position;\n    private final CacheType cacheType;\n\n    @FunctionalInterface\n    public interface CacheQueryHandler {\n        QueryResult<?> apply(\n            final Query<?> query,\n            final Position mergedPosition,\n            final PositionBound positionBound,\n            final QueryConfig config,\n            final StateStore store\n        );\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"rawtypes\")\n    private final Map<Class, CacheQueryHandler> queryHandlers =\n        mkMap(\n            mkEntry(\n                KeyQuery.class,\n                (query, mergedPosition, positionBound, config, store) ->\n                    runKeyQuery(query, mergedPosition, positionBound, config)\n            )\n        );\n\n\n    CachingKeyValueStore(final KeyValueStore<Bytes, byte[]> underlying, final CacheType cacheType) {\n        super(underlying);\n        position = Position.emptyPosition();\n        this.cacheType = cacheType;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void init(final StateStoreContext stateStoreContext, final StateStore root) {\n        internalContext = asInternalProcessorContext(stateStoreContext);\n        cacheName = ThreadCache.nameSpaceFromTaskIdAndStore(internalContext.taskId().toString(), name());\n        internalContext.registerCacheFlushListener(cacheName, entries -> {\n            for (final ThreadCache.DirtyEntry entry : entries) {\n                putAndMaybeForward(entry, internalContext);\n            }\n        });\n        super.init(stateStoreContext, root);\n        // save the stream thread as we only ever want to trigger a flush\n        // when the stream thread is the current thread.\n        streamThread = Thread.currentThread();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Position getPosition() {\n        // We return the merged position since the query uses the merged position as well\n        final Position mergedPosition = Position.emptyPosition();\n        final Position wrappedPosition = wrapped().getPosition();\n        synchronized (position) {\n            synchronized (wrappedPosition) {\n                mergedPosition.merge(position);\n                mergedPosition.merge(wrappedPosition);\n            }\n        }\n        return mergedPosition;\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    @Override\n    public <R> QueryResult<R> query(final Query<R> query,\n                                    final PositionBound positionBound,\n                                    final QueryConfig config) {\n\n        if (cacheType == CacheType.TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE_WITH_HEADERS) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\"Queries (IQv2) are not supported for timestamped key-value stores with headers yet.\");\n        }\n\n        final long start = config.isCollectExecutionInfo() ? System.nanoTime() : -1L;\n        final QueryResult<R> result;\n\n        final CacheQueryHandler handler = queryHandlers.get(query.getClass());\n        if (handler == null) {\n            result = wrapped().query(query, positionBound, config);\n\n        } else {\n            final int partition = internalContext.taskId().partition();\n            final Lock lock = this.lock.readLock();\n            lock.lock();\n            try {\n                validateStoreOpen();\n                final Position mergedPosition = getPosition();\n\n                // We use the merged position since the cache and the store may be at different positions\n                if (!StoreQueryUtils.isPermitted(mergedPosition, positionBound, partition)) {\n                    result = QueryResult.notUpToBound(mergedPosition, positionBound, partition);\n                } else {\n                    result = (QueryResult<R>) handler.apply(\n                        query,\n                        mergedPosition,\n                        positionBound,\n                        config,\n                        this\n                    );\n                }\n            } finally {\n                lock.unlock();\n            }\n        }\n        if (config.isCollectExecutionInfo()) {\n            result.addExecutionInfo(\n                \"Handled in \" + getClass() + \" in \" + (System.nanoTime() - start) + \"ns\");\n        }\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private <R> QueryResult<R> runKeyQuery(final Query<R> query,\n                                           final Position mergedPosition,\n                                           final PositionBound positionBound,\n                                           final QueryConfig config) {\n        if (cacheType == CacheType.TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE_WITH_HEADERS) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\"Queries (IQv2) are not supported for timestamped key-value stores with headers yet.\");\n        }\n        QueryResult<R> result = null;\n        final KeyQuery<Bytes, byte[]> keyQuery = (KeyQuery<Bytes, byte[]>) query;\n\n        if (keyQuery.isSkipCache()) {\n            return wrapped().query(query, positionBound, config);\n        }\n\n        final Bytes key = keyQuery.getKey();\n\n        synchronized (mergedPosition) {\n            if (internalContext.cache() != null) {\n                final LRUCacheEntry lruCacheEntry = internalContext.cache().get(cacheName, key);\n                if (lruCacheEntry != null) {\n                    final byte[] rawValue;\n                    if (cacheType == CacheType.TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE && !WrappedStateStore.isTimestamped(wrapped()) && !StoreQueryUtils.isAdapter(wrapped())) {\n                        rawValue = ValueAndTimestampDeserializer.rawValue(lruCacheEntry.value());\n                    } else {\n                        rawValue = lruCacheEntry.value();\n                    }\n                    result = (QueryResult<R>) QueryResult.forResult(rawValue);\n                }\n            }\n\n            // We don't need to check the position at the state store since we already performed the check on\n            // the merged position above\n            if (result == null) {\n                result = wrapped().query(query, PositionBound.unbounded(), config);\n            }\n            result.setPosition(mergedPosition.copy());\n        }\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    private void putAndMaybeForward(final ThreadCache.DirtyEntry entry,\n                                    final InternalProcessorContext<?, ?> context) {\n        if (flushListener != null) {\n            final byte[] rawNewValue = entry.newValue();\n            final byte[] rawOldValue = rawNewValue == null || sendOldValues ? wrapped().get(entry.key()) : null;\n\n            // this is an optimization: if this key did not exist in underlying store and also not in the cache,\n            // we can skip flushing to downstream as well as writing to underlying store\n            if (rawNewValue != null || rawOldValue != null) {\n                // we need to get the old values if needed, and then put to store, and then flush\n                final ProcessorRecordContext current = context.recordContext();\n                try {\n                    context.setRecordContext(entry.entry().context());\n                    wrapped().put(entry.key(), entry.newValue());\n                    flushListener.apply(\n                        new Record<>(\n                            entry.key().get(),\n                            new Change<>(rawNewValue, sendOldValues ? rawOldValue : null),\n                            entry.entry().context().timestamp(),\n                            entry.entry().context().headers()));\n                } finally {\n                    context.setRecordContext(current);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            final ProcessorRecordContext current = context.recordContext();\n            try {\n                context.setRecordContext(entry.entry().context());\n                wrapped().put(entry.key(), entry.newValue());\n            } finally {\n                context.setRecordContext(current);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean setFlushListener(final CacheFlushListener<byte[], byte[]> flushListener,\n                                    final boolean sendOldValues) {\n        this.flushListener = flushListener;\n        this.sendOldValues = sendOldValues;\n\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void put(final Bytes key,\n                    final byte[] value) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(key, \"key cannot be null\");\n        validateStoreOpen();\n        lock.writeLock().lock();\n        try {\n            validateStoreOpen();\n            // for null bytes, we still put it into cache indicating tombstones\n            putInternal(key, value);\n        } finally {\n            lock.writeLock().unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void putInternal(final Bytes key,\n                             final byte[] value) {\n        synchronized (position) {\n            internalContext.cache().put(\n                cacheName,\n                key,\n                new LRUCacheEntry(\n                    value,\n                    internalContext.recordContext().headers(),\n                    true,\n                    internalContext.recordContext().offset(),\n                    internalContext.recordContext().timestamp(),\n                    internalContext.recordContext().partition(),\n                    internalContext.recordContext().topic(),\n                    internalContext.recordContext().sourceRawKey(),\n                    internalContext.recordContext().sourceRawValue()\n                )\n            );\n\n            StoreQueryUtils.updatePosition(position, internalContext);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public byte[] putIfAbsent(final Bytes key,\n                              final byte[] value) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(key, \"key cannot be null\");\n        validateStoreOpen();\n        lock.writeLock().lock();\n        try {\n            validateStoreOpen();\n            final byte[] v = getInternal(key);\n            if (v == null) {\n                putInternal(key, value);\n            }\n            return v;\n        } finally {\n            lock.writeLock().unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void putAll(final List<KeyValue<Bytes, byte[]>> entries) {\n        validateStoreOpen();\n        lock.writeLock().lock();\n        try {\n            validateStoreOpen();\n            for (final KeyValue<Bytes, byte[]> entry : entries) {\n                Objects.requireNonNull(entry.key, \"key cannot be null\");\n                put(entry.key, entry.value);\n            }\n        } finally {\n            lock.writeLock().unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public byte[] delete(final Bytes key) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(key, \"key cannot be null\");\n        validateStoreOpen();\n        lock.writeLock().lock();\n        try {\n            validateStoreOpen();\n            return deleteInternal(key);\n        } finally {\n            lock.writeLock().unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private byte[] deleteInternal(final Bytes key) {\n        final byte[] v = getInternal(key);\n        putInternal(key, null);\n        return v;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public byte[] get(final Bytes key) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(key, \"key cannot be null\");\n        validateStoreOpen();\n        final Lock theLock;\n        if (Thread.currentThread().equals(streamThread)) {\n            theLock = lock.writeLock();\n        } else {\n            theLock = lock.readLock();\n        }\n        theLock.lock();\n        try {\n            validateStoreOpen();\n            return getInternal(key);\n        } finally {\n            theLock.unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private byte[] getInternal(final Bytes key) {\n        LRUCacheEntry entry = null;\n        if (internalContext.cache() != null) {\n            entry = internalContext.cache().get(cacheName, key);\n        }\n        if (entry == null) {\n            final byte[] rawValue = wrapped().get(key);\n            if (rawValue == null) {\n                return null;\n            }\n            // only update the cache if this call is on the streamThread\n            // as we don't want other threads to trigger an eviction/flush\n            if (Thread.currentThread().equals(streamThread)) {\n                internalContext.cache().put(cacheName, key, new LRUCacheEntry(rawValue));\n            }\n            return rawValue;\n        } else {\n            return entry.value();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public KeyValueIterator<Bytes, byte[]> range(final Bytes from,\n                                                 final Bytes to) {\n        if (Objects.nonNull(from) && Objects.nonNull(to) && from.compareTo(to) > 0) {\n            LOG.warn(\"Returning empty iterator for fetch with invalid key range: from > to. \" +\n                \"This may be due to range arguments set in the wrong order, \" +\n                \"or serdes that don't preserve ordering when lexicographically comparing the serialized bytes. \" +\n                \"Note that the built-in numerical serdes do not follow this for negative numbers\");\n            return KeyValueIterators.emptyIterator();\n        }\n\n        validateStoreOpen();\n        final KeyValueIterator<Bytes, byte[]> storeIterator = wrapped().range(from, to);\n        final ThreadCache.MemoryLRUCacheBytesIterator cacheIterator = internalContext.cache().range(cacheName, from, to);\n        return new MergedSortedCacheKeyValueBytesStoreIterator(cacheIterator, storeIterator, true);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public KeyValueIterator<Bytes, byte[]> reverseRange(final LOG.warn(\"Returning empty iterator for fetch with invalid key range: from > to. \" +\n                \"This may be due to range arguments set in the wrong order, \" +\n                \"or serdes ThreadCache.MemoryLRUCacheBytesIterator cacheIterator = internalContext.cache().reverseRange(cacheName, from, to);\n        return new MergedSortedCacheKeyValueBytesStoreIterator(cacheIterator, Bytes from = Bytes.wrap(prefixKeySerializer.serialize(null, prefix));\n        final Bytes to = Bytes.increment(from);\n        final ThreadCache.MemoryLRUCacheBytesIterator cacheIterator = Let me trace through `query()` more carefully—it acquires the readLock first, then calls `getPosition()` which synchronizes on position and wrappedPosition. The `runKeyQuery` method synchronizes on `mergedPosition`, a local variable returned by `getPosition()`, so that's a fresh object each time and shouldn't cause issues.\n\nIn `put()`, the writeLock is acquired first, then `putInternal()` synchronizes on position. But readLock and writeLock on the same ReentrantReadWriteLock can't deadlock against each other—writeLock just blocks until readLock releases.\n\nThe real issue might be in the `commit()`/`flushCache()` path: `commit()` acquires writeLock, then calls `cache().flush()` which triggers `putAndMaybeForward()` and eventually `wrapped().put()`. During the flush operation, something is likely acquiring locks in a conflicting order.\n\nLooking at `getPosition()`, it acquires `position` first, then `wrappedPosition` (which comes from the underlying store). The key question is whether anything locks `wrappedPosition` before `position`—that would create a deadlock cycle. I need to trace through the code more carefully to find where that reverse lock ordering might occur. The wrapped store's position object is the same reference, so when `commit()` or `flushCache()` triggers the flush listener and calls `wrapped().put()`, it might synchronize on that same position object. But the lock ordering still doesn't cycle because the outer store acquires its write lock before reaching the wrapped store's position, while `getPosition()` goes the opposite direction—so I need to trace through more carefully where the actual deadlock occurs.\n\nLooking at the specific code paths: `query()` grabs the read lock then synchronizes on position and wrapped position; `put()` grabs the write lock then synchronizes on position; `commit()` grabs the write lock and triggers a flush that calls `wrapped().put()`; and `get()` on a non-stream thread grabs the read lock and might do something in `getInternal()`. Let me map out exactly where the circular wait happens.\n\nThread 1 holds the read lock and is waiting for the position lock, while Thread 2 holds the position lock and is waiting for the write lock—but that can't create a deadlock since the write lock would block Thread 1 from ever acquiring the read lock in the first place. I need to find the actual cycle that's causing the hang.\n\nLet me trace through whether the wrapped store could be another CachingKeyValueStore instance. If `wrapped()` returns a CachingKeyValueStore, then calling `getPosition()` on it would try to acquire its own position lock, and if that wrapped store also calls back into this store somehow, that could create a circular dependency.\n\nLooking at `DefaultStateUpdater` more carefully—it seems to be acquiring multiple locks in inconsistent orders across different code paths, which is likely where the actual deadlock is occurring.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7255,"cache_read_input_tokens":19120,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":7255,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"1d2b1ffa-0ec5-4400-b7bc-6a360e46f83a","uuid":"2e8c86c0-4d1e-40d3-b89e-f155318554de"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01F8Lba6BH3crutoDNw9YyWt","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Looking at the DefaultStateUpdater, `executeWithQueuesLocked()` acquires all three locks in order: tasksAndActionsLock -> restoredActiveTasksLock -> exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock. The individual methods each acquire only one lock at a time. I don't see a code path in DefaultStateUpdater where locks are acquired in a reverse or different order. The inner thread methods also acquire only one lock at a time.\n\nSo the DefaultStateUpdater might not actually have a deadlock issue since the individual lock acquisitions don't nest.\n\nLet me go back and focus on the `CachingKeyValueStore`. The `getPosition()` method has nested `synchronized` blocks:\n\n\nNested lock acquisition...\n\nThese don't conflict since there's no reverse ordering. But what if the wrapped store is itself a CachingKeyValueStore? If we have layered caching stores where the outer store has its own position lock and the inner wrapped store has another, then when the outer store calls `getPosition()` it would acquire its position lock first, then call the wrapped store's `getPosition()` which acquires the wrapped store's position lock. This creates a potential deadlock scenario if the inner store ever tries to access the outer store's locks in reverse order. this calls inner.getPosition() which does `synchronized(position_inner) { synchronized(inner_wrappedPosition) {...} }`\n2. Then `synchronized(position_outer) { synchronized(wrappedPosition) {...} }` where `wrappedPosition` is the same `position_inner` object\n\nSo the order is: position_inner -> ... -> position_outer -> position_inner\n\nThat's a cycle! But... `wrapped().getPosition()` is called before the synchronized blocks, so the inner locks would already be released. Actually no, let me re-read the code—it's getting the wrapped position first, then locking the outer position and the wrapped position together. So `wrappedPosition` is actually a reference to the inner store's position object, not a copy.\n\nNow I need to trace through what happens when the inner store calls `putInternal()` or other methods—if those also try to lock their position while the outer store is holding locks on both positions, that could create a deadlock scenario. any method locks `inner.position`, and some code path first locks `inner.position` then tries to lock `outer.position`, we'd have a cycle.\n\nBut these are separate stores, and the outer store wraps the inner one. The outer store calls into the inner store, not the other way around.\n\nHmm, let me reconsider. The real question is: is there some path where:\n- Thread 1 holds lock A and waits for lock B\n- Thread 2 holds lock B and waits for lock A\n\nThe readLock/writeLock is a `ReentrantReadWriteLock`. readLock and writeLock can interact:\n- Multiple threads can hold readLock simultaneously\n- writeLock is exclusive\n- If a thread holds readLock, another thread trying to get writeLock will block\n- If a thread holds writeLock, other threads trying to get readLock will block\n\nLet me trace through a specific scenario in `CachingKeyValueStore`. If one thread acquires the readLock and then tries to grab the position monitor, while another thread acquires the writeLock and also needs the position monitor, the second thread would actually be blocked waiting for the readLock to release first—so there's no circular wait there. But what if I flip it: a thread takes the writeLock and then tries to acquire the position monitor...\n\nActually, I think the issue might be with the `internalContext.cache()` call triggering something that tries to acquire a lock that's already held elsewhere.\n\nLet me search more systematically through the codebase for places where synchronized methods or blocks call into other synchronized code paths, since that's where circular lock dependencies typically hide.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1329,"cache_read_input_tokens":34841,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1329,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"1d2b1ffa-0ec5-4400-b7bc-6a360e46f83a","uuid":"cfdc02dd-ca37-438e-865f-305b2d376bcd"}

   private final class StreamStateListener implements StreamThread.StateListener {\n        private final Map<Long, StreamThread.State> threadState;\n        private GlobalStreamThread.State globalThreadState;\n\n        StreamStateListener(final GlobalStreamThread.State globalThreadState) {\n            this.threadState = new HashMap<>();\n            this.globalThreadState = globalThreadState;\n        }\n\n        /**\n         * If all threads are up, including the global thread, set to RUNNING\n         */\n        private void maybeSetRunning() {\n            // state can be transferred to RUNNING if\n            // 1) all threads are either RUNNING or DEAD\n            // 2) thread is pending-shutdown and there are still other threads running\n            final boolean hasRunningThread = threadState.values().stream().anyMatch(s -> s == StreamThread.State.RUNNING);\n            for (final StreamThread.State state : threadState.values()) {\n                if (state == StreamThread.State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN && hasRunningThread) continue;\n                if (state != StreamThread.State.RUNNING && state != StreamThread.State.DEAD) {\n                    return;\n                }\n            }\n\n            // the global state thread is relevant only if it is started. There are cases\n            // when we don't have a global state thread at all, e.g., when we don't have global KTables\n            if (globalThreadState != null && globalThreadState != GlobalStreamThread.State.RUNNING) {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            setState(State.RUNNING);\n        }\n\n\n        @Override\n        public synchronized void onChange(final Thread thread,\n                                          final ThreadStateTransitionValidator abstractNewState,\n                                          final ThreadStateTransitionValidator abstractOldState) {\n            // StreamThreads first\n            if (thread instanceof StreamThread) {\n                final StreamThread.State newState = (StreamThread.State) abstractNewState;\n                threadState.put(thread.getId(), newState);\n\n                if (newState == StreamThread.State.PARTITIONS_REVOKED || newState == StreamThread.State.PARTITIONS_ASSIGNED) {\n                    setState(State.REBALANCING);\n                } else if (newState == StreamThread.State.RUNNING) {\n                    maybeSetRunning();\n                }\n            } else if (thread instanceof GlobalStreamThread) {\n                // global stream thread has different invariants\n                final GlobalStreamThread.State newState = (GlobalStreamThread.State) abstractNewState;\n                globalThreadState = newState;\n\n                if (newState == GlobalStreamThread.State.RUNNING) {\n                    maybeSetRunning();\n                } else if (newState == GlobalStreamThread.State.DEAD) {\n                    if (state != State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN) {\n                        log.error(\"Global thread has died. The streams application or client will now close to ERROR.\");\n                        closeToError();\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        private synchronized void registerStreamThread(final StreamThread streamThread) {\n            threadState.put(streamThread.getId(), streamThread.state());\n        }\n    }\n\n    static final class DelegatingStateRestoreListener implements StateRestoreListener {\n        private StateRestoreListener userStateRestoreListener;\n\n        private void throwOnFatalException(final Exception fatalUserException,\n                                           final TopicPartition topicPartition,\n                                           final String storeName) {\n            throw new StreamsException(\n                    String.format(\"Fatal user code error in store restore listener for store %s, partition %s.\",\n                            storeName,\n                            topicPartition),\n                    fatalUserException);","numLines":80,"startLine":614,"totalLines":2154}}}

Let me search through the codebase for places where both locks are involved in the opposite order.\n\nActually, I'm realizing I should trace through the StreamStateListener callback more carefully. When StreamThread changes state, it triggers onChange which calls back into KafkaStreams.setState, and I need to map out exactly when each lock is held during this sequence to see if there's a circular dependency. I've identified the first lock ordering: StreamThread.stateLock → Tasks monitor. Now I need to find the reverse path—where Tasks monitor is held first and then StreamThread.stateLock is accessed—to confirm a deadlock cycle. Let me trace through TaskManager to see if acquiring the Tasks monitor could lead back to StreamThread state access, or if there's another lock involved like the ReadWriteLock in CachingKeyValueStore that might be part of the cycle.\n\nLooking at the CachingKeyValueStore operations more carefully, I'm seeing multiple lock orderings: put() acquires the write lock then synchronizes on position, query() takes the read lock and then synchronizes on both position and wrappedPosition, and commit/flushCache operations also use the write lock before flushing the cache. These nested synchronizations could be creating the deadlock under concurrent operations. The key question is what `wrapped().getPosition()` actually returns—whether it's a direct reference to the inner store's position field or a copy. Looking at how `query()` acquires locks, it grabs the read lock first, then synchronizes on both the outer and inner position objects during `getPosition()`. The real deadlock risk seems to be in how `put()` from the flush callback might interact with these position locks when called from a different thread than the one executing `query()`.\n\nLet me trace through the other caching store implementations to see if they follow the same synchronization pattern and whether any of them expose a deadlock scenario.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":318,"cache_read_input_tokens":48241,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":318,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"1d2b1ffa-0ec5-4400-b7bc-6a360e46f83a","uuid":"21916972-1beb-4c75-b633-e277c21265cb"}

       if (log.isTraceEnabled()) {\n   124→            log.trace(\"Named cache {} stats on flush: #hits={}, #misses={}, #overwrites={}, #flushes={}\",\n   125→                name, hits(), misses(), overwrites(), flushes());\n   126→        }\n   127→\n   128→        if (listener == null) {\n   129→            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"No listener for namespace \" + name + \" registered with cache\");\n   130→        }\n   131→\n   132→        if (dirtyKeys.isEmpty()) {\n   133→            return;\n   134→        }\n   135→\n   136→        final it to the list of\n   140→        // flushed entries and remove from dirtyKeys.\n   141→        if (evicted != null) {\n   142→            entries.add(new ThreadCache.DirtyEntry(evicted.key, evicted.entry.value(), evicted.entry));\n   143→            dirtyKeys.remove(evicted.key);\n   144→        }\n   145→\n   146→        for (final Bytes key : dirtyKeys) {\n   147→            final LRUNode node = getInternal(key);\n   148→            if (node == null) {\n   149→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Key = \" + key + \" found in dirty key set, but entry is null\");\n   150→            }\n   151→            entries.add(new ThreadCache.DirtyEntry(key, node.entry.value(), node.entry));\n   152→            node.entry.markClean();\n   153→            if (node.entry.value() == null) {\n   154→                deleted.add(node.key);\n   155→            }\n   156→        }\n   157→        // clear dirtyKeys before the listener is applied as it may be re-entrant.\n   158→        dirtyKeys.clear();\n   159→        listener.apply(entries);\n   160→        for (final Bytes key : deleted) {\n   161→            delete(key);\n   162→        }\n   163→    }\n   164→\n   165→    synchronized void put(final Bytes key, final LRUCacheEntry value) {\n   166→        if (!value.isDirty() && dirtyKeys.contains(key)) {\n   167→            throw new IllegalStateException(\n   168→                String.format(\n   169→                    \"Attempting to put a clean entry for key [%s] into NamedCache [%s] when it already contains a dirty entry for the same key\",\n   170→                    key, name\n   171→                )\n   172→            );\n   173→        }\n   174→        LRUNode node = cache.get(key);\n   175→        if (node != null) {\n   176→            numOverwrites++;\n   177→\n   178→            currentSizeBytes -= node.size();\n   179→            node.update(value);\n   180→            updateLRU(node);\n   181→           hitRatioSensor.record((double) numReadHits / (double) (numReadHits + numReadMisses));\n   209→        }\n   210→        return node;\n   211→    }\n   212→\n   213→    private void updateLRU(final LRUNode node) {\n   214→        remove(node);\n   215→\n   216→        putHead(node);\n   217→    }\n   218→\n   219→    private void remove(final LRUNode node) {\n   220→        if (node.previous != null) {\n   221→            node.previous.next = node.next;\n   222→        } else {\n   223→            head = node.next;\n   224→        }\n   225→        if (node.next != null) {\n   226→            node.next.previous = node.previous;\n   227→        } else {\n   228→            tail = node.previous;\n   229→        }\n   230→    }\n   231→\n   232→    private void  238→        head = node;\n   239→        if (tail == null) {\n   240→            tail = head;\n   241→        }\n   242→    }\n   243→\n   244→    synchronized void evict() {\n   245→        if (tail == null) {\n   246→            return;\n   247→        }\n   248→        final LRUNode eldest = tail;\n   249→        currentSizeBytes -= eldest.size();\n   250→        remove(eldest);\n   251→        cache.remove(eldest.key);\n   252→        if (eldest.entry.isDirty()) {\n   253→            flush(eldest);\n   254→        }\n   255→        totalCacheSizeSensor.record(currentSizeBytes);\n   256→ 313→        } else {\n   314→            return cache.navigableKeySet().subSet(from, true, to, toInclusive);\n   315→        }\n   316→    }\n   317→\n   318→    private Iterator<Bytes>   public interface CacheQueryHandler {\n    72→        QueryResult<?> apply(\n    73→            final Query<?> query,\n    74→            final Position mergedPosition,\n    75→            final @SuppressWarnings(\"rawtypes\")\n    82→    private final Map<Class, CacheQueryHandler> queryHandlers =\n    83→        mkMap(\n    84→            mkEntry(\n    85→                KeyQuery.class,\n    86→                (query, mergedPosition, positionBound, config, store) ->\n    87→                    runKeyQuery(query, mergedPosition, positionBound, config)\n    88→            )\n    89→ StateStore root) {\n   100→        internalContext = asInternalProcessorContext(stateStoreContext);\n   101→        cacheName = ThreadCache.nameSpaceFromTaskIdAndStore(internalContext.taskId().toString(), name());\n   102→        internalContext.registerCacheFlushListener(cacheName, entries -> {\n   103→            for (final
