position as well\n   116→        final Position mergedPosition = Position.emptyPosition();\n   117→        final Position wrappedPosition = wrapped().getPosition();\n   118→        synchronized (position) {\n   119→            synchronized (wrappedPosition) {\n   120→                mergedPosition.merge(position);\n   121→                mergedPosition.merge(wrappedPosition);\n   122→            }\n   123→        }\n   124→        return mergedPosition;\n   125→    }\n   126→\n   127→    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   128→    @Override\n   129→    public <R> QueryResult<R> query(final Query<R> query,\n   130→                                    final PositionBound positionBound,\n   131→                                    final QueryConfig config) {\n   132→\n   133→        if (cacheType == CacheType.TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE_WITH_HEADERS) {\n   134→            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\"Queries (IQv2) are not supported for timestamped key-value stores with headers yet.\");\n   135→        }\n   136→\n   137→        final long start = config.isCollectExecutionInfo() ? System.nanoTime() : -1L;\n   138→        final QueryResult<R> result;\n   139→\n   140→        final CacheQueryHandler handler = queryHandlers.get(query.getClass());\n   141→        if (handler == null) {\n   142→            result = wrapped().query(query, positionBound, config);\n   143→\n   144→        } else {\n   145→            final int partition = internalContext.taskId().partition();\n   146→            final Lock lock = this.lock.readLock();\n   147→            lock.lock();\n   148→            try {\n   149→                validateStoreOpen();\n   150→                final Position mergedPosition = getPosition();\n   151→\n   152→                // We use the merged position since the cache and the store may be at different positions\n   153→                if (!StoreQueryUtils.isPermitted(mergedPosition, positionBound, partition)) {\n   154→                    result = QueryResult.notUpToBound(mergedPosition, positionBound, partition);\n   155→                } else {\n   156→                    result = (QueryResult<R>) handler.apply(\n   157→                        query,\n   158→                        mergedPosition,\n   159→                        positionBound,\n   160→                        config,\n   161→                        this\n   162→                    );\n   163→                }\n   164→            } finally {\n   165→                lock.unlock();\n   166→            }\n   167→        }\n   168→        if (config.isCollectExecutionInfo()) {\n   169→            result.addExecutionInfo(\n   170→                \"Handled in \" + getClass() + \" in \" + (System.nanoTime() - start) + \"ns\");\n   171→        }\n   172→        return result;\n   173→    }\n   174→\n   175→    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   176→    private <R> QueryResult<R> runKeyQuery(final Query<R> query,\n   177→                                           final Position mergedPosition,\n   178→                                           final PositionBound positionBound,\n   179→                                           final QueryConfig config) {\n   180→        if (cacheType == CacheType.TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE_WITH_HEADERS) {\n   181→            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\"Queries (IQv2) are not supported for timestamped key-value stores with headers yet.\");\n   182→        }\n   183→        QueryResult<R> result = null;\n   184→        final KeyQuery<Bytes, byte[]> keyQuery = (KeyQuery<Bytes, byte[]>) query;\n   185→\n   186→        if (keyQuery.isSkipCache()) {\n   187→            return wrapped().query(query, positionBound, config);\n   188→        }\n   189→\n   190→        final Bytes key = keyQuery.getKey();\n   191→\n   192→        synchronized (mergedPosition) {\n   193→            if (internalContext.cache() != null) {\n   194→                final LRUCacheEntry lruCacheEntry = internalContext.cache().get(cacheName, key);\n   195→                if (lruCacheEntry != null) {\n   196→                    final byte[] rawValue;\n   197→                    if (cacheType == CacheType.TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE && !WrappedStateStore.isTimestamped(wrapped()) && !StoreQueryUtils.isAdapter(wrapped())) {\n   198→                        rawValue = ValueAndTimestampDeserializer.rawValue(lruCacheEntry.value());\n   199→                    } else {\n   200→                        rawValue = lruCacheEntry.value();\n   201→                    }\n   202→                    result = (QueryResult<R>) QueryResult.forResult(rawValue);\n   203→                }\n   204→            }\n   205→\n   206→            // We don't need to check the position at the state store since we already performed the check on\n   207→            // the merged position above\n   208→            if (result == null) {\n   209→                result = wrapped().query(query, PositionBound.unbounded(), config);\n   210→            }\n   211→            result.setPosition(mergedPosition.copy());\n   212→        }\n   213→        return result;\n   214→    }\n   215→\n   216→    private void putAndMaybeForward(final ThreadCache.DirtyEntry entry,\n   217→                                    final InternalProcessorContext<?, ?> context) {\n   218→        if (flushListener != null) {\n   219→            final byte[] rawNewValue = entry.newValue();\n   220→            final byte[] rawOldValue = rawNewValue == null || sendOldValues ? wrapped().get(entry.key()) : null;\n   221→\n   222→            // this is an optimization: if this key did not exist in underlying store and also not in the cache,\n   223→            // we can skip flushing to downstream as well as writing to underlying store\n   224→            if (rawNewValue != null || rawOldValue != null) {\n   225→                // we need to get the old values if needed, and then put to store, and then flush\n   226→                final ProcessorRecordContext current = context.recordContext();\n   227→                try {\n   228→                    context.setRecordContext(entry.entry().context());\n   229→                    wrapped().put(entry.key(), entry.newValue());\n   230→                    flushListener.apply(\n   231→                        new Record<>(\n   232→                            entry.key().get(),\n   233→                            new Change<>(rawNewValue, sendOldValues ? rawOldValue : null),\n   234→                            entry.entry().context().timestamp(),\n   235→                            entry.entry().context().headers()));\n   236→                } finally {\n   237→                    context.setRecordContext(current);\n   238→                }\n   239→            }\n   240→        } else {\n   241→            final ProcessorRecordContext current = context.recordContext();\n   242→            try {\n   243→                context.setRecordContext(entry.entry().context());\n   244→                wrapped().put(entry.key(), entry.newValue());\n   245→            } finally {\n   246→                context.setRecordContext(current);\n   247→            }\n   248→        }\n   249→    }\n   250→\n   251→    @Override\n   252→    public boolean setFlushListener(final CacheFlushListener<byte[], byte[]> flushListener,\n   253→                                    final boolean sendOldValues) {\n   254→        this.flushListener = flushListener;\n   255→        this.sendOldValues = sendOldValues;\n   256→\n   257→        return true;\n   258→    }\n   259→\n   260→    @Override\n   261→    public void put(final Bytes key,\n   262→                    final byte[] value) {\n   263→        Objects.requireNonNull(key, \"key cannot be null\");\n   264→ 504→                () -> internalContext.cache().flush(cacheName),\n   505→                () -> internalContext.cache().close(cacheName),\n   506→                wrapped()::close\n   507→            );\n   508→            if (!suppressed.isEmpty()) {\n   509→                throwSuppressed(\"Caught an exception while closing caching key value store for store \" + name(),\n   510→ not\n    37→ * record based\n    38→ */\n    39→public class ThreadCache {\n    40→    private final Logger log;\n    41→    private volatile long maxCacheSizeBytes;\n    42→    private final StreamsMetricsImpl metrics;\n    43→    private final Map<String, NamedCache> caches = new HashMap<>();\n    44→\n    45→    // Invariant: equal to sum of sizeInBytes of caches\n    46→    private final AtomicLong sizeInBytes = new AtomicLong();\n    47→\n    48→    // internal stats\n    49→    private long numPuts = 0;\n    50→    private long numGets = 0;\n    51→    private long numEvicts = 0;\n    52→    private long numFlushes = 0;\n    53→\n    54→    public interface DirtyEntryFlushListener {\n    55→        void apply(final List<DirtyEntry> dirty);\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    public ThreadCache(final LogContext logContext, final long maxCacheSizeBytes, final StreamsMetricsImpl metrics) {\n    59→        this.maxCacheSizeBytes = maxCacheSizeBytes;\n    60→        this.metrics = metrics;\n    61→        this.log = logContext.logger(getClass());\n    62→    }\n    63→\n    64→    public long puts() {\n    65→        return numPuts;\n    66→    }\n    67→\n    68→    public long gets() {\n    69→        return numGets;\n    70→    }\n    71→\n    72→    public long evicts() {\n    73→        return numEvicts;\n    74→    }\n    75→\n    76→    public long flushes() {\n    77→        return numFlushes;\n    78→    }\n    79→\n    80→    public synchronized void resize(final long newCacheSizeBytes) {\n    81→        final boolean shrink = newCacheSizeBytes < maxCacheSizeBytes;\n    82→        maxCacheSizeBytes = newCacheSizeBytes;\n    83→        if (shrink) {\n    84→            log.debug(\"Cache size was shrunk to {}\", newCacheSizeBytes);\n    85→            if (caches.values().isEmpty()) {\n    86→                return;\n    87→            }\n    88→            final CircularIterator<NamedCache> circularIterator = new CircularIterator<>(caches.values());\n    89→            while (sizeInBytes.get() > maxCacheSizeBytes) {\n    90→                final NamedCache cache = circularIterator.next();\n    91→                synchronized (cache) {\n    92→                    final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n    93→                    cache.evict();\n    94→                    sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(cache.sizeInBytes() - oldSize);\n    95→                }\n    96→                numEvicts++;\n    97→            }\n    98→        } else {\n    99→            log.debug(\"Cache size was expanded to {}\", newCacheSizeBytes);\n   100→        }\n   101→    }\n   102→\n   103→    /**\n   104→     * The thread cache maintains a set of {@link NamedCache}s whose names are a concatenation of the task ID and the\n   105→     * underlying store name. This method creates those names.\n   106→     * @param taskIDString Task ID\n   107→     * @param underlyingStoreName Underlying store name\n   108→     */\n   109→    public static String nameSpaceFromTaskIdAndStore(final String taskIDString, final String underlyingStoreName) {\n   110→        return taskIDString + \"-\" + underlyingStoreName;\n   111→    }\n   112→\n   113→    /**\n   114→     * Given a cache name of the form taskid-storename, return the task ID.\n   115→     */\n   116→    public static String taskIDfromCacheName(final String cacheName) {\n   117→        final */\n   133→    public void addDirtyEntryFlushListener(final String namespace, final DirtyEntryFlushListener listener) {\n   134→        final NamedCache cache = getOrCreateCache(namespace);\n   135→        cache.setListener(listener);\n   136→    }\n   137→\n   138→    public void flush(final String namespace) {\n   139→        numFlushes++;\n   140→\n   141→        final NamedCache cache = getCache(namespace);\n   142→        if (cache == null) {\n   143→            return;\n   144→        }\n   145→\n   146→        synchronized (cache) {\n   147→            final long oldSize = {\n   153→            log.trace(\"Cache stats on flush: #puts={}, #gets={}, #evicts={}, #flushes={}\", puts(), gets(), evicts(), flushes());\n   154→        }\n   155→    }\n   156→\n   157→    public LRUCacheEntry get(final String namespace, final Bytes key) {\n   158→        numGets++;\n   159→\n   160→        if (key == null) {\n   161→            return null;\n   162→        }\n   163→\n   164→        final NamedCache cache = getCache(namespace);\n   165→        if (cache == null) {\n   166→            return null;\n   167→        }\n   168→        return cache.get(key);\n   169→    }\n   170→\n   171→    public void put(final String namespace, final Bytes key, final LRUCacheEntry value) {\n   172→        numPuts++;\n   173→\n   174→        final NamedCache cache = getOrCreateCache(namespace);\n   175→\n   176→        synchronized (cache) {\n   177→            final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n   178→            cache.put(key, value);\n   179→ String namespace, final Bytes key, final LRUCacheEntry value) {\n   185→        final NamedCache cache = getOrCreateCache(namespace);\n   186→        final LRUCacheEntry result;\n   187→        synchronized (cache) {\n   188→            final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n   189→            result = cache.putIfAbsent(key, value);\n   190→            sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(cache.sizeInBytes() - oldSize);\n   191→            maybeEvict(namespace, cache);\n   192→        }\n   193→\n   194→        if (result == null) {\n   195→        for (final KeyValue<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry> entry : entries) {\n   202→            put(namespace, entry.key, entry.value);\n   203→        }\n   204→    }\n   205→\n   206→    public    }\n   254→        return new MemoryLRUCacheBytesIterator(cache.reverseAllKeys(), cache);\n   255→    }\n   256→\n   257→    public long size() {\n   258→        long size = 0;\n   259→        for (final NamedCache cache : caches.values()) {\n   260→            size += cache.size();\n   261→            if (isOverflowing(size)) {\n   262→                return Long.MAX_VALUE;\n   263→            }\n   264→        }\n   265→        return size;\n   266→    }\n   267→\n   268→    private boolean isOverflowing(final long size) {\n   269→        return size < 0;\n   270→    }\n   271→\n   272→    long sizeBytes() {\n   273→        return sizeInBytes.get();\n   274→    }\n   275→\n   276→    synchronized void close(final String namespace) {\n   277→        final NamedCache removed = caches.remove(namespace);\n   278→        if (removed != null) {\n   279→            sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(-removed.sizeInBytes());\n   280→            removed.close();\n   281→        }\n   282→    }\n   283→\n   284→    synchronized void clear(final String namespace) {\n   285→        final NamedCache cleared = caches.get(namespace);\n   286→        if (cleared != null) {\n   287→            sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(-cleared.sizeInBytes());\n   288→            cleared.clear();\n   289→        }\n   290→    }\n   291→\n   292→    private void maybeEvict(final String namespace, final NamedCache cache) {\n   293→        int numEvicted = 0;\n   294→        while (sizeInBytes.get() > maxCacheSizeBytes) {\n   295→            // we abort here as the put on this cache may have triggered\n   296→            // a put on another cache. So even though the sizeInBytes() is\n   297→            // still > maxCacheSizeBytes there is nothing to evict from this\n   298→            // namespaced cache.\n   299→            if (cache.isEmpty()) {\n   300→                return;\n   301→            }\n   302→            final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n   303→            cache.evict();\n   304→            sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(cache.sizeInBytes() - oldSize);\n   305→            numEvicts++;\n   306→            numEvicted++;\n   307→        }\n   308→        if (log.isTraceEnabled()) {\n   309→            log.trace(\"Evicted {} entries from cache {}\", numEvicted, namespace);\n   310→        }\n   311→    }\n   312→\n   313→    private synchronized NamedCache getCache(final String namespace) {\n   314→        return caches.get(namespace);\n   315→    }\n   316→\n   317→    private synchronized NamedCache getOrCreateCache(final String name) {\n   318→        NamedCache cache = caches.get(name);\n   319→        if (cache == null) {\n   320→            cache = new NamedCache(name, this.metrics);\n   321→            caches.put(name, cache);\n   322→        }\n   323→        return cache;\n   324→    }\n   325→\n   326→    static class MemoryLRUCacheBytesIterator implements PeekingKeyValueIterator<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry> {\n   327→        private final Iterator<Bytes> keys;\n   328→        private final NamedCache cache;\n   329→        private KeyValue<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry> nextEntry;\n   330→\n   331→        MemoryLRUCacheBytesIterator(final Iterator<Bytes> keys, final NamedCache cache) {\n   332→            this.keys = keys;\n   333→            this.cache = cache;\n   334→        }\n   335→\n   336→        public Bytes peekNextKey() {\n   337→            if (!hasNext()) {\n   338→                throw new NoSuchElementException();\n   339→            }\n   340→            return nextEntry.key;\n   341→        }\n   342→\n   343→\n   344→        public KeyValue<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry> peekNext() {\n   345→            if (!hasNext()) {\n   346→                throw new NoSuchElementException();\n   347→            }\n   348→            return nextEntry;\n   349→        }\n   350→\n   351→        @Override\n   352→        public boolean hasNext() {\n   353→            if (nextEntry != null) {\n   354→                return true;\n   355→            }\n   356→\n   357→            while (keys.hasNext() && nextEntry == null) {\n   358→                internalNext();\n   359→            }\n   360→\n   361→            return nextEntry != LRUCacheEntry> peekNext() {\n   428→            if (!hasNext()) {\n   429→                throw new NoSuchElementException();\n   430→            }\n   431→            return current.peekNext();\n   432→        }\n   433→\n   434→        @Override\n   435→        public KeyValue<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry> next() {\n   436→            if (!hasNext()) {\n   437→                throw new NoSuchElementException();\n   438→            }\n   439→            return current.next();\n   440→        }\n   441→\n   442→        @Override\n   443→        public void close() {\n   444→            current.close();\n   445→        }\n   446→\n   447→        private long currentSegmentBeginTime() {\n   448→            return currentSegmentId * segmentInterval;\n   449→        }\n   450→\n   451→        private long currentSegmentLastTime() {\n   452→            return currentSegmentBeginTime() + segmentInterval - 1;\n   453→        }\n   454→\n   455→        private void getNextSegmentIterator() {\n   456→            if (forward) {\n   457→                ++currentSegmentId;\n   458→                lastSegmentId = cacheFunction.segmentId(maxObservedTimestamp);\n   459→\n   460→                if (currentSegmentId > lastSegmentId) {\n   461→                    current = null;\n   462→                    return;\n   463→                }\n   464→\n   465→                setCacheKeyRange(currentSegmentBeginTime(), currentSegmentLastTime());\n   466→\n   467→                current.close();\n   468→\n   469→                current = internalContext.cache().range(cacheName, cacheKeyFrom, cacheKeyTo);\n   470→            } else {\n   471→                --currentSegmentId;\n   472→\n   473→                if (currentSegmentId < lastSegmentId) {\n   474→                    current = null;\n   475→                    return;\n   476→                }\n   477→\n   478→                setCacheKeyRange(currentSegmentBeginTime(), currentSegmentLastTime());\n   479→\n   480→                current.close();\n   481→\n   482→                current = internalContext.cache().reverseRange(cacheName, cacheKeyFrom, cacheKeyTo);\n   483→            }\n   484→\n   485→        }\n   486→\n   487→        private void setCacheKeyRange(final long lowerRangeEndTime, final long upperRangeEndTime) {\n   488→            if (cacheFunction.segmentId(lowerRangeEndTime) != cacheFunction.segmentId(upperRangeEndTime)) {\n   489→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Error iterating over segments: segment interval has changed\");\n   490→            }\n   491→\n   492→            if (keyFrom.equals(keyTo)) {\n   493→                cacheKeyFrom = cacheFunction.cacheKey(segmentLowerRangeFixedSize(keyFrom, lowerRangeEndTime));\n   494→                cacheKeyTo = cacheFunction.cacheKey(segmentUpperRangeFixedSize(keyTo, upperRangeEndTime));\n   495→            } else {\n   496→                cacheKeyFrom = cacheFunction.cacheKey(keySchema.lowerRange(keyFrom, lowerRangeEndTime), currentSegmentId);\n   497→                cacheKeyTo = cacheFunction.cacheKey(keySchema.upperRange(keyTo, latestSessionStartTime), currentSegmentId);\n   498→            }\n   499→        }\n   500→\n   501→        private Bytes segmentLowerRangeFixedSize(final Bytes key, final long segmentBeginTime) {\n   502→            final Windowed<Bytes> sessionKey = new Windowed<>(key, new SessionWindow(0, Math.max(0, segmentBeginTime)));\n   503→            return SessionKeySchema.toBinary(sessionKey);\n   504→        }\n   505→\n   506→        private Bytes segmentUpperRangeFixedSize(final Bytes key, final long segmentEndTime) {\n   507→            final Windowed<Bytes> sessionKey = new Windowed<>(key, new SessionWindow(Math.min(latestSessionStartTime, segmentEndTime), segmentEndTime));\n   508→            return SessionKeySchema.toBinary(sessionKey);\n   509→        }\n   510→    }\n   511→}\n ThreadCache.DirtyEntry entry,\n    96→                                    final InternalProcessorContext<?, ?> context) {\n    97→        final byte[] binaryWindowKey = 119→                            entry.entry().context().timestamp(),\n   120→                            entry.entry().context().headers()));\n   121→                } finally {\n   122→                    context.setRecordContext(current);\n   123→                }\n   124→            }\n   125→        } else {\n   126→            final ProcessorRecordContext current = context.recordContext();\n        private long currentSegmentLastTime() {\n   543→            return Math.min(timeTo, currentSegmentBeginTime() + segmentInterval - 1);\n   544→        }\n   545→\n   546→        private void 550→                lastSegmentId = cacheFunction.segmentId(Math.min(timeTo, maxObservedTimestamp.get()));\n   551→\n   552→                if (currentSegmentId > lastSegmentId) {\n   553→ null) {\n   156→                    continue;\n   157→                }\n   158→\n   159→                finalEntry = new DirtyEntry(entry.key(), value.value(), value);\n   160→\n   161→                if (hasIndex) {\n   162→                    processedBasedKey.add(baseKey);\n   163→                }\n   164→            } else {\n   165→                final Bytes baseKey = Bytes.wrap(binaryWindowKey);\n   166→                if (hasIndex && processedBasedKey.contains(baseKey)) {\n   167→                    // Processed in index\n   168→                    continue;\n   169→                }\n   170→                finalEntry = entry;\n   171→                if (hasIndex) {\n   172→                    processedBasedKey.add(Bytes.wrap(binaryWindowKey));\n   173→                }\n   174→            }\n   175→\n   176→            final Windowed<Bytes> windowedKeyBytes;\n   177→            if (isBaseKey) {\n   178→                windowedKeyBytes = TimeFirstWindowKeySchema.fromStoreBytesKey(binaryWindowKey,\n   179→                    windowSize);\n   180→            } else {\n   181→                windowedKeyBytes = KeyFirstWindowKeySchema.fromStoreBytesKey(binaryWindowKey,\n   182→                    windowSize);\n   183→            }\n   184→\n   185→            final long windowStartTimestamp = windowedKeyBytes.window().start();\n   186→            final Bytes binaryKey = windowedKeyBytes.key();\n   187→\n   188→            putAndMaybeForward(context, finalEntry, binaryKey, windowStartTimestamp);\n   189→        }\n   190→    }\n   191→\n   192→    private void putAndMaybeForward(final InternalProcessorContext<?, ?> context,\n   193→                                    final DirtyEntry finalEntry,\n   194→                                    final Bytes binaryKey,\n   195→                                    final long windowStartTimestamp) {\n   196→        if (flushListener != null) {\n   197→            final byte[] rawNewValue = finalEntry.newValue();\n   198→            final byte[] rawOldValue = rawNewValue == null || sendOldValues ?\n   199→                wrapped().fetch(binaryKey, windowStartTimestamp) : null;\n   200→\n   201→            // this is an optimization: if this key did not exist in underlying store and also not in the cache,\n   202→            // we can skip flushing to downstream as well as writing to underlying store\n   203→            if (rawNewValue != null || rawOldValue != null) {\n   204→                // we need to get the old values if needed, and then put to store, and then flush\n   205→                final ProcessorRecordContext current = context.recordContext();\n   206→                try {\n   207→                    context.setRecordContext(finalEntry.entry().context());\n   208→                    wrapped().put(binaryKey, finalEntry.newValue(), windowStartTimestamp);\n   209→\n   210→                    flushListener.apply(\n   211→                        new Record<>(\n   212→                            WindowKeySchema.toStoreKeyBinary(binaryKey,\n   213→                                    windowStartTimestamp, 0)\n   214→                                .get(),\n   215→                            new Change<>(rawNewValue, sendOldValues ? rawOldValue : null),\n   216→                            finalEntry.entry().context().timestamp(),\n   217→                            finalEntry.entry().context().headers()));\n   218→                } finally {\n   219→                    context.setRecordContext(current);\n   220→                }\n   221→            }\n   222→        } else {\n   223→            final ProcessorRecordContext current = context.recordContext();\n   224→            try {\n   225→                context.setRecordContext(finalEntry.entry().context());\n   226→                wrapped().put(binaryKey, finalEntry.newValue(), windowStartTimestamp);\n   227→            } finally {\n   228→                context.setRecordContext(current);\n   229→            }\n   230→        }\n   231→    }\n   232→\n   233→    @Override\n   234→    public boolean setFlushListener(final CacheFlushListener<byte[], byte[]> flushListener,\n   235→                                    final boolean sendOldValues) {\n   236→        this.flushListener = flushListener;\n   237→        this.sendOldValues = sendOldValues;\n   238→\n   239→        return true;\n   240→    }\n   241→\n   242→    private Bytes indexKeyToBaseKey(final Bytes indexKey) {\n   243→        final byte[] key = KeyFirstWindowKeySchema.extractStoreKeyBytes(indexKey.get());\n   244→        final long timestamp = KeyFirstWindowKeySchema.extractStoreTimestamp(indexKey.get());\n   245→        final int seqnum = KeyFirstWindowKeySchema.extractStoreSequence(indexKey.get());\n   246→        return TimeFirstWindowKeySchema.toStoreKeyBinary(key, timestamp, seqnum);\n   247→    }\n   248→\n   249→    @Override\n   250→    public synchronized void put(final Bytes key,\n   251→                                 final byte[] value,\n   252→                                 final long windowStartTimestamp) {\n   253→        // since this function may not access the underlying inner store, we need to validate\n   254→        // if store is open outside as well.\n   255→        validateStoreOpen();\n   256→\n   257→        final Bytes baseKeyBytes = TimeFirstWindowKeySchema.toStoreKeyBinary(key, windowStartTimestamp, 0);\n   258→        final LRUCacheEntry entry =\n   259→            new LRUCacheEntry(\n   260→                value,\n   261→                internalContext.recordContext().headers(),\n   262→                true,\n   263→                internalContext.recordContext().offset(),\n   264→                internalContext.recordContext().timestamp(),\n   265→                internalContext.recordContext().partition(),\n   266→                internalContext.recordContext().topic(),\n   267→                internalContext.recordContext().sourceRawKey(),\n   268→                internalContext.recordContext().sourceRawValue()\n   269→            );\n   270→\n   271→        // Put to index first so that base can be evicted later\n   272→        if (hasIndex) {\n   273→            // Important: put base key first to avoid the situation that if we put index first,\n   274→            // it could be evicted when we are putting base key. In that case, base key is not yet\n   275→            // in cache so we can't store key/value to store when index is evicted. Then if we fetch\n   276→            // using index, we can't find it in either store or cache\n   277→            internalContext.cache().put(cacheName, baseKeyCacheFunction.cacheKey(baseKeyBytes), 282→                    true,\n   283→                    internalContext.recordContext().offset(),\n   284→                    internalContext.recordContext().timestamp(),\n   285→                    internalContext.recordContext().partition(),\n   286→                    \"\",\n   287→                    internalContext.recordContext().sourceRawKey(),\n   288→                    internalContext.recordContext().sourceRawValue()\n   289→                );\n   290→            final Bytes indexKey = KeyFirstWindowKeySchema.toStoreKeyBinary(key, windowStartTimestamp, 0);\n   291→            internalContext.cache().put(cacheName, indexKeyCacheFunction.cacheKey(indexKey), emptyEntry);\n   292→        } else {\n   293→            internalContext.cache().put(cacheName, baseKeyCacheFunction.cacheKey(baseKeyBytes), entry);\n   294→        }\n   295→        maxObservedTimestamp.set(Math.max(windowStartTimestamp, maxObservedTimestamp.get()));\n   296→    }\n   297→\n   298→    @Override\n   299→    public byte[] fetch(final Bytes key,\n   300→                        final long timestamp) {\n   301→        validateStoreOpen();\n   302→        if (internalContext.cache() == null) {\n   303→            return wrapped().fetch(key, timestamp);\n   304→        }\n   305→\n   306→        final Bytes baseBytesKey = 535→    }\n   536→\n   537→\n   538→    private class CacheIteratorWrapper implements PeekingKeyValueIterator<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry> {\n   539→\n   540→        private final long segmentInterval;\n   541→        private final Bytes keyFrom;\n   542→        private final Bytes keyTo;\n   543→        private final long timeTo;\n   544→        private final boolean forward;\n   545→        private cacheKeyFrom;\n   550→        private Bytes cacheKeyTo;\n   551→        private LRUCacheEntry cachedBaseValue;\n   552→        private final SegmentedCacheFunction cacheFunction;\n   553→\n   554→        private ThreadCache.MemoryLRUCacheBytesIterator current;\n   555→\n   556→        private CacheIteratorWrapper(final Bytes key,\n   557→                                     final long timeFrom,\n 561→            this(key, key, timeFrom, timeTo, forward, index);\n   562→        }\n   563→\n   564→        private CacheIteratorWrapper(final Bytes keyFrom,\n   565→                                     final Bytes keyTo,\n   566→                                     final long timeFrom,\n   567→                                     final long timeTo,\n   568→ baseKeyCacheFunction;\n   577→\n   578→            this.segmentInterval = cacheFunction.getSegmentInterval();\n   579→\n   580→            if (forward) {\n   581→                this.lastSegmentId = cacheFunction.segmentId(Math.min(timeTo, maxObservedTimestamp.get()));\n   582→                this.currentSegmentId = cacheFunction.segmentId(timeFrom);\n   583→\n   584→                setCacheKeyRange(timeFrom, currentSegmentLastTime());\n   585→                this.current = internalContext.cache().range(cacheName, cacheKeyFrom, cacheKeyTo);\n   586→            } else {\n   587→                this.currentSegmentId = cacheFunction.segmentId(Math.min(timeTo, maxObservedTimestamp.get()));\n   588→                this.lastSegmentId = cacheFunction.segmentId(timeFrom);\n   589→\n   590→                setCacheKeyRange(currentSegmentBeginTime(), Math.min(timeTo, maxObservedTimestamp.get()));\n   591→                this.current = internalContext.cache().reverseRange(cacheName, cacheKeyFrom, cacheKeyTo);\n   592→            }\n   593→        }\n   594→\n   595→        @Override\n   596→        public boolean hasNext() {\n   597→            if (current == null) {\n   598→                return false;\n   599→            }\n   600→\n   601→            if (useIndex) {\n   602→                do {\n   603→                    // If iterating from index, need to make sure base key/value exist in cache\n   604→                    while (current.hasNext()) {\n   605→                        final Bytes cacheIndexKey = current.peekNextKey();\n   606→                        final Bytes indexKey = indexKeyCacheFunction.key(cacheIndexKey);\n   607→                        final Bytes baseKey = indexKeyToBaseKey(indexKey);\n   608→                        final Bytes cachedBaseKey = baseKeyCacheFunction.cacheKey(baseKey);\n   609→                        cachedBaseValue = internalContext.cache().get(cacheName, cachedBaseKey);\n   610→                        if (cachedBaseValue != null) {\n   611→                            return true;\n   612→                        }\n   613→                        current.next();\n   614→                    }\n   615→                    getNextSegmentIterator();\n   616→                } while (current != null);\n   617→                return false;\n   618→            }\n   619→\n   620→            if (current.hasNext()) {\n   621→                return true;\n   622→            }\n   623→\n   624→            while (!current.hasNext()) {\n   625→                getNextSegmentIterator();\n   626→                if (current == null) {\n   627→                    return false;\n   628→                }\n   629→            }\n   630→            return true;\n   631→        }\n   632→\n   633→        @Override\n   634→        public Bytes peekNextKey() {\n   635→            if (!hasNext()) {\n   636→                throw new NoSuchElementException();\n   637→            }\n   638→            return current.peekNextKey();\n   639→        }\n   640→\n   641→        @Override\n   642→        public KeyValue<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry> peekNext() {\n   643→            if (!hasNext()) {\n   644→                throw new NoSuchElementException();\n   645→            }\n   646→            if (useIndex) {\n   647→                final KeyValue<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry> kv = current.peekNext();\n   648→                return KeyValue.pair(kv.key, cachedBaseValue);\n   649→            }\n   650→            return current.peekNext();\n   651→        }\n   652→\n   653→        @Override\n   654→        public KeyValue<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry> next() {\n   655→            if (!hasNext()) {\n   656→                throw new NoSuchElementException();\n   657→            }\n   658→            if (useIndex) {\n   659→                final KeyValue<Bytes, LRUCacheEntry> kv = current.next();\n   660→                return KeyValue.pair(kv.key, cachedBaseValue);\n   661→            }\n   662→            return current.next();\n   663→        }\n   664→\n   665→        @Override\n   666→        public void close() {\n   667→            current.close();\n   668→        }\n   669→\n   670→        private long currentSegmentBeginTime() {\n   671→            return currentSegmentId * segmentInterval;\n   672→        }\n   673→\n   674→        private long currentSegmentLastTime() {\n   675→            return Math.min(timeTo, currentSegmentBeginTime() + segmentInterval - 1);\n   676→        }\n   677→\n   678→        private void getNextSegmentIterator() {\n   679→            if (forward) {\n   680→                ++currentSegmentId;\n   681→                // updating as maxObservedTimestamp can change while iterating\n   682→                lastSegmentId = cacheFunction.segmentId(Math.min(timeTo, maxObservedTimestamp.get()));\n   683→\n   684→                if (currentSegmentId > lastSegmentId) {\n   685→                    current = null;\n   686→                    return;\n   687→                }\n   688→\n   689→                setCacheKeyRange(currentSegmentBeginTime(), currentSegmentLastTime());\n   690→\n   691→                current.close();\n   692→\n   693→                current = internalContext.cache().range(cacheName, cacheKeyFrom, cacheKeyTo);\n   694→            } else {\n   695→                --currentSegmentId;\n   696→\n   697→                // last segment id is stable when iterating backward, therefore no need to update\n   698→                if (currentSegmentId < lastSegmentId) {\n   699→                    current = null;\n   700→                    return;\n   701→                }\n   702→\n   703→                setCacheKeyRange(currentSegmentBeginTime(), currentSegmentLastTime());\n   704→\n   705→                current.close();\n   706→\n   707→                current = internalContext.cache().reverseRange(cacheName, cacheKeyFrom, cacheKeyTo);\n   708→            }\n   709→        }\n   710→\n   711→        private void setCacheKeyRange(final long lowerRangeEndTime, final long upperRangeEndTime) {\n   712→            if (cacheFunction.segmentId(lowerRangeEndTime) != cacheFunction.segmentId(upperRangeEndTime)) {\n   713→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Error iterating over segments: segment interval has changed\");\n   714→            }\n   715→\n   716→            final KeySchema schema = useIndex ? indexKeySchema : baseKeySchema;\n   717→\n   718→            if (keyFrom != null && keyFrom.equals(keyTo)) {\n   719→                cacheKeyFrom = cacheFunction.cacheKey(schema.lowerRangeFixedSize(keyFrom, lowerRangeEndTime), currentSegmentId);\n   720→                cacheKeyTo = cacheFunction.cacheKey(schema.upperRangeFixedSize(keyTo, upperRangeEndTime), currentSegmentId);\n   721→            } else {\n   722→                cacheKeyFrom = cacheFunction.cacheKey(schema.lowerRange(keyFrom, lowerRangeEndTime), currentSegmentId);\n   723→                cacheKeyTo = cacheFunction.cacheKey(schema.upperRange(keyTo, timeTo), {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_0192F7s4Hgi6udbQNoZYdGCP","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"The agent's analysis suggests a deadlock between CachingKeyValueStore's ReadWriteLock and NamedCache monitor. But let me verify this more carefully.\n\nThe key question: When the NamedCache eviction callback fires (holding NamedCache monitor), does `putAndMaybeForward()` try to reacquire the CachingKeyValueStore's writeLock?\n\nLooking at `putAndMaybeForward()` (lines 216-248), it calls `wrapped().put()`. The wrapped store is the underlying store (not the CachingKeyValueStore itself). So it doesn't try to reacquire the CachingKeyValueStore's lock.\n\n\nBut here's the critical issue: eviction happens inside `synchronized(cache)` within `ThreadCache.put()`, and `CachingKeyValueStore.putInternal()` calls this while holding both writeLock and synchronized(position). The real question is whether a separate thread holding a NamedCache monitor can independently try to acquire the CachingKeyValueStore's writeLock, since eviction can affect entries across multiple NamedCaches.\n\nActually, looking more carefully at the eviction logic - it only evicts from the same cache that was just written to, not from arbitrary caches. The eviction's flush listener then triggers `putAndMaybeForward()` which calls into the wrapped store, but this all happens while the NamedCache monitor is already held.\n\nBut `resize()` works differently - it iterates through all caches in the ThreadCache, acquiring each NamedCache monitor individually while holding the ThreadCache's own monitor. This means any cache's eviction callback could potentially trigger a flush listener that tries to reacquire locks in a different order.\n\nActually, looking at the call chain more carefully: when ThreadCache.put() triggers eviction, it goes through synchronized blocks on the cache, then to the flush listener, which calls putAndMaybeForward() on the wrapped store - but that doesn't need CachingKeyValueStore's lock, so there's no circular dependency there either.\n\nI need to step back and reconsider where the actual deadlock is occurring, since the CachingKeyValueStore case doesn't seem to create the lock cycle I initially thought it did.\n\nWait, I'm seeing the real issue now: ThreadCache.resize() holds its monitor while acquiring NamedCache monitors, but ThreadCache.put() releases the ThreadCache monitor before acquiring the NamedCache monitor, so there's no deadlock risk between those two operations.\n\nHowever, if a flush listener triggered during resize() tries to acquire the ThreadCache monitor while resize() is holding it, that could cause a deadlock. But the wrapped store shouldn't interact with ThreadCache directly.\n\nLet me step back and look at this more systematically—maybe there's a known deadlock pattern in the codebase or test cases that reveal the actual issue.\n\nActually, rereading the problem: the hang is non-deterministic and depends on thread scheduling, which suggests concurrent operations. In Kafka Streams, that's typically the StreamThread doing processing (put, get, commit, flush) while user threads perform interactive queries. Since put() acquires a write lock and query()/get() acquire read locks, those shouldn't deadlock directly—but there might be something else I'm missing.\n\nWait, I'm noticing something in putAll(): it acquires the write lock, then calls put() inside the loop, which tries to acquire the write lock again. That's a reentrant lock issue—if the lock isn't reentrant, this would deadlock immediately.\n\nActually, ReentrantReadWriteLock handles this fine since the same thread can acquire the lock multiple times. So the deadlock must be coming from somewhere else. Let me trace through the synchronization more carefully—maybe it's the interaction between the position monitor and the ReadWriteLock that's causing the problem.\n\nLooking at the call chains, `getPosition()` acquires the position monitor first, then wrappedPosition, while `put()` acquires the writeLock first and then tries to acquire the position monitor inside `putInternal()`. The question is whether there's a path where the position monitor gets acquired before the lock, which would create a cycle.\n\nNow I'm examining whether multiple CachingKeyValueStore instances sharing the same ThreadCache could create cross-store deadlocks—specifically, whether Store A's eviction listener could trigger operations on Store B that lead back to Store A while holding different locks in a conflicting order.\n\nLet me trace through the resize() method and look more carefully at CachingWindowStore's synchronized blocks to see if there's a circular lock dependency I'm missing.\n\nWhen commit() runs, it acquires the write lock, then calls cache().flush() which synchronizes on the ThreadCache and the individual cache object before invoking the listener that does the actual put. Meanwhile, put() also needs the write lock, so those operations serialize properly. But query() uses a read lock instead — I need to check if that creates a window where a deadlock could occur.\n\nActually, I'm realizing the deadlock might not be in CachingKeyValueStore itself. Let me look more carefully at DefaultTaskManager since that was mentioned in the exploration report.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4893,"cache_read_input_tokens":58538,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4893,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":9,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"1d2b1ffa-0ec5-4400-b7bc-6a360e46f83a","uuid":"312e2fd9-d784-4029-88d0-84937deb210f"}

DefaultTaskManager implements TaskManager {\n    55→\n    56→    private final Time time;\n    57→    private final Logger log;\n    58→    private final TasksRegistry tasks;\n    59→\n    60→    private final Lock tasksLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    61→    private final Condition tasksCondition = tasksLock.newCondition();\n    62→    private final List<TaskId> lockedTasks = new ArrayList<>();\n    63→    private final Map<TaskId, StreamsException> uncaughtExceptions = new HashMap<>();\n    64→    private final Map<TaskId, TaskExecutor> assignedTasks = new HashMap<>();\n    65→    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n    66→\n    67→    private final List<TaskExecutor> taskExecutors;\n    68→\n    69→    public static class 76→    public DefaultTaskManager(final Time time,\n    77→                              final String clientId,\n    78→                              final TasksRegistry tasks,\n    79→                              final TaskExecutorCreator executorCreator,\n    80→                              final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata,\n    81→                              final int numExecutors\n    82→                              ) {\n    83→        final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", clientId);\n    84→        final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n    85→        this.log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskManager.class);\n    86→        this.time = time;\n    87→        this.tasks = tasks;\n    88→        this.taskExecutionMetadata = taskExecutionMetadata;\n    89→\n    90→        this.taskExecutors = new ArrayList<>(numExecutors);\n    91→        for (int i = 1; i <= numExecutors; i++) {\n    92→            final String name = clientId + \"-TaskExecutor-\" + i;\n    93→            this.taskExecutors.add(executorCreator.create(this, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata));\n    94→        }\n    95→    }\n    96→\n    97→    @Override\n    98→    public StreamTask assignNextTask(final TaskExecutor executor) {\n    99→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   100→            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n   101→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for getting next task to assign is unrecognized\");\n   102→            }\n   103→\n   104→            // the most naive scheduling algorithm for now: give the next unlocked, unassigned, and  processable task\n   105→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   106→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   107→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   108→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   109→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   110→                ) {\n   111→\n   112→                    assignedTasks.put(task.id(), executor);\n   113→\n   114→                    log.debug(\"Assigned task {} to executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n   115→\n   116→                    return task;\n   117→                }\n   118→            }\n   119→\n   120→            log.debug(\"Found no assignable task for executor {}\", executor.name());\n   121→\n   122→            return null;\n   123→        });\n   124→    }\n   125→\n   126→    @Override\n   127→    public void awaitProcessableTasks(final Supplier<Boolean> isShuttingDown) throws InterruptedException {\n   128→        final boolean interrupted = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   129→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   130→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   131→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   132→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   133→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   134→                ) {\n   135→                    log.debug(\"Await unblocked: returning early from await since a processable task {} was found\", task.id());\n   136→                    return false;\n   137→                }\n   138→            }\n   139→            try {\n   140→                // We re-check the shutdownRequest atomic boolean to avoid a race condition. If this thread was\n   141→                // previously interrupted while awaiting tasksCondition, it is possible to miss the signalAll that\n   142→                // is called during shutdown. If this happens, we end up blocking in the await forever.\n   143→                if (!isShuttingDown.get()) {\n   144→                    log.debug(\"Await blocking\");\n   145→                    tasksCondition.await();\n   146→                } else {\n   147→                    log.debug(\"Not awaiting since shutdown was requested\");\n   148→                }\n   149→            } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n   150→                // we interrupt the thread for shut down and pause.\n   151→                // we can ignore this exception.\n   152→                log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Interrupted while waiting for processable tasks\");\n   153→                return true;\n   154→            }\n   155→            log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Woken up to check for processable tasks\");\n   156→            return false;\n   157→        });\n   158→\n   159→        if (interrupted) {\n   160→            throw new InterruptedException();\n   161→        }\n   162→    }\n   163→\n   164→    public void signalTaskExecutors() {\n   165→        log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   166→        executeWithTasksLocked(tasksCondition::signalAll);\n   167→    }\n   168→\n   169→    @Override\n   170→    public void unassignTask(final StreamTask task, final TaskExecutor executor) {\n   171→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   172→ 176→            final TaskExecutor lockedExecutor = assignedTasks.get(task.id());\n   177→            if (lockedExecutor == null || lockedExecutor != executor) {\n   178→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Task \" + task.id() + \" is not locked by the executor\");\n   179→            }\n   180→\n   181→            assignedTasks.remove(task.id());\n   182→\n   183→            log.debug(\"Unassigned {} from executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n   184→            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n   185→        });\n   186→    }\n   187→\n   188→    @Override\n   189→    public KafkaFuture<Void> lockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n   190→        final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> result = new KafkaFutureImpl<>();\n   191→\n   192→        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   193→            result.complete(null);\n   194→            return result;\n   195→        }\n   196→\n   197→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   198→            lockedTasks.addAll(taskIds);\n Task task = tasks.initializedTask(taskId);\n   204→\n   205→                if (task == null) {\n   206→                    throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Trying to lock task \" + taskId + \" but it's not owned\");\n   207→                }\n   208→\n   209→                if (!task.isActive()) {\n   210→                    throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The locking task \" + taskId + \" is not an active task\");\n   211→                }\n   212→\n   213→                if (assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   214→                    final TaskExecutor executor = assignedTasks.get(taskId);\n   215→                    log.debug(\"Requesting release of task {} from {}\", taskId, executor.name());\n   216→                    final KafkaFuture<StreamTask> future = executor.unassign();\n   217→                    future.whenComplete((streamTask, throwable) -> {\n   218→                        if (throwable != null) {\n   219→                            result.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n   220→                        } else {\n   221→                            assert !assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId);\n   222→                            // It can happen that the executor handed back the task before we asked it to\n   223→                            // in which case `streamTask` will be null here.\n   224→                            assert streamTask == null || streamTask.id() == taskId;\n   225→                            remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n   226→                            if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   227→                                result.complete(null);\n   228→                            }\n   229→                        }\n   230→                    });\n   231→                } else {\n   232→                    remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n   233→                    if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   234→                        result.complete(null);\n   235→                    }\n   236→                }\n   237→            }\n   238→\n   239→            return result;\n   240→        });\n   241→    }\n   242→\n   243→    @Override\n   244→    public KafkaFuture<Void> );\n   248→    }\n   249→\n   250→    @Override\n   251→    public void unlockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n   252→\n   253→        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   254→            return;\n   255→        }\n   256→\n   257→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   258→            lockedTasks.removeAll(taskIds);\n   259→            log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   260→    if (!lockedTasks.contains(taskId)) {\n   290→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is not locked yet by the task manager\");\n   291→            }\n   292→\n   293→            if (!tasks.containsInitialized(taskId)) {\n   294→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is not owned by the task manager\");\n   295→            }\n   296→\n   297→            tasks.removeTask(tasks.initializedTask(taskId));\n   298→        });\n   299→\n   300→        log.info(\"Removed task {} from the task manager\", taskId);\n   301→    }\n   302→\n   303→    @Override\n   304→    public Set<ReadOnlyTask> getTasks() {\n   305→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(ReadOnlyTask::new).collect(Collectors.toSet()));\n   306→    }\n   307→\n   308→    @Override\n   309→    public void setUncaughtException(final StreamsException exception, final TaskId taskId) {\n   310→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   311→\n   312→            if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   313→                throw {\n   337→            log.debug(\"Drained {} uncaught exceptions\", returnValue.size());\n   338→        }\n   339→\n   340→        return returnValue;\n   341→    }\n   342→\n   343→    public executeWithTasksLocked(final Runnable action) {\n   348→        tasksLock.lock();\n   349→        try {\n   350→            action.run();\n   351→        } finally {\n   352→            tasksLock.unlock();\n   353→        }\n   354→    }\n   355→\n   356→    private <T> T returnWithTasksLocked(final Supplier<T> action) {\n   357→        tasksLock.lock();\n   358→        try {\n   359→            return action.get();\n   360→        } finally {\n   361→            tasksLock.unlock();\n   362→        }\n   363→    }\n   364→\n   365→    private boolean canProgress(final StreamTask task, final long nowMs) {\n   366→        return\n   367→            taskExecutionMetadata.canProcessTask(task, nowMs) && task.isProcessable(nowMs) ||\n   368→                taskExecutionMetadata.canPunctuateTask(task) && (task.canPunctuateStreamTime() || task.canPunctuateSystemTime());\n   369→    }\n   370→\n   371→    public void startTaskExecutors() {\n   372→        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n   373→            t.start();\n   374→        }\n   375→    }\n   376→\n   377→    public void shutdown(final Duration duration) {\n   378→        for This is possible if we do not have enough executors or because those tasks\n *    are not processable (e.g. because no records fetched) yet.\n */\npublic final class DefaultTaskManager implements name, final Time time, final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata) {\n            return new DefaultTaskExecutor(taskManager, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata);\n        }\n    }\n\n    public DefaultTaskManager(final Time time,\n                              final String clientId,\n                              final TasksRegistry tasks,\n                              final TaskExecutorCreator executorCreator,\n                              final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata,\n                              final int numExecutors\n                              ) {\n        final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", clientId);\n        final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n        this.log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskManager.class);\n        this.time = time;\n        this.tasks = tasks;\n        this.taskExecutionMetadata = taskExecutionMetadata;\n\n        this.taskExecutors = new ArrayList<>(numExecutors);\n        for (int i = 1; i <= numExecutors; i++) {\n            final String name = clientId + \"-TaskExecutor-\" + i;\n            this.taskExecutors.add(executorCreator.create(this, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata));\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public StreamTask assignNextTask(final TaskExecutor executor) {\n        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for getting next task to assign is unrecognized\");\n            }\n\n            // the most naive scheduling algorithm for now: give the next unlocked, unassigned, and  processable task\n            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n                ) {\n\n                    assignedTasks.put(task.id(), executor);\n\n                    log.debug(\"Assigned task {} to executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n\n                    return task;\n                }\n            }\n\n            log.debug(\"Found no assignable task for executor {}\", executor.name());\n\n            return null;\n        });\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void awaitProcessableTasks(final Supplier<Boolean> isShuttingDown) throws InterruptedException {\n        final boolean interrupted = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n                ) {\n                    log.debug(\"Await unblocked: returning early from await since a processable task {} was found\", task.id());\n                    return false;\n                }\n            }\n            try {\n                // We re-check the shutdownRequest atomic boolean to avoid a race condition. If this thread was\n                // previously interrupted while awaiting tasksCondition, it is possible to miss the signalAll that\n                // is called during shutdown. If this happens, we end up blocking in the await forever.\n                if (!isShuttingDown.get()) {\n                    log.debug(\"Await blocking\");\n                    tasksCondition.await();\n                } else {\n                    log.debug(\"Not awaiting since shutdown was requested\");\n                }\n            } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n                // we interrupt the thread for shut down and pause.\n                // we can ignore this exception.\n                log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Interrupted while waiting for processable tasks\");\n                return true;\n            }\n            log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Woken up to check for processable tasks\");\n            return false;\n        });\n\n        if (interrupted) {\n            throw new InterruptedException();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void signalTaskExecutors() {\n        log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n        executeWithTasksLocked(tasksCondition::signalAll);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void unassignTask(final StreamTask task, final TaskExecutor executor) {\n        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for unassign task is unrecognized\");\n            }\n\n            final TaskExecutor lockedExecutor = assignedTasks.get(task.id());\n            if (lockedExecutor == null || lockedExecutor != executor) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Task \" + task.id() + \" is not locked by the executor\");\n            }\n\n            assignedTasks.remove(task.id());\n\n            log.debug(\"Unassigned {} from executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n        });\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public KafkaFuture<Void> lockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n        final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> result = new KafkaFutureImpl<>();\n\n        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n            result.complete(null);\n            return result;\n        }\n\n        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            lockedTasks.addAll(taskIds);\n\n            final Set<TaskId> remainingTaskIds = new ConcurrentSkipListSet<>(taskIds);\n\n            for (final TaskId taskId : taskIds) {\n                final Task task = tasks.initializedTask(taskId);\n\n                if (task == null) {\n                    throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Trying to lock task \" + taskId + \" but it's not owned\");\n                }\n\n                if (!task.isActive()) {\n                    throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The locking task \" + taskId + \" is not an active task\");\n                }\n\n                if (assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n                    final TaskExecutor executor = assignedTasks.get(taskId);\n                    log.debug(\"Requesting release of task {} from {}\", taskId, executor.name());\n                    final KafkaFuture<StreamTask> future = executor.unassign();\n                    future.whenComplete((streamTask, throwable) -> {\n                        if (throwable != null) {\n                            result.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n                        } else {\n                            assert !assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId);\n                            // It can happen that the executor handed back the task before we asked it to\n                            // in which case `streamTask` will be null here.\n                            assert streamTask == null || streamTask.id() == taskId;\n                            remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n                            if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n                                result.complete(null);\n                            }\n                        }\n                    });\n                } else {\n                    remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n                    if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n                        result.complete(null);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            return result;\n        });\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public KafkaFuture<Void> lockAllTasks() {\n        return returnWithTasksLocked(() ->\n            lockTasks(tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(Task::id).collect(Collectors.toSet()))\n        );\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void unlockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n\n        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            lockedTasks.removeAll(taskIds);\n            log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n        });\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void unlockAllTasks() {\n        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> unlockTasks(tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(Task::id).collect(Collectors.toSet())));\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void add(final Set<StreamTask> tasksToAdd) {\n        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            for (final StreamTask task : tasksToAdd) {\n                tasks.addActiveTask(task);\n            }\n            log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n        });\n\n        log.info(\"Added tasks {} to the task manager to process\", tasksToAdd);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void remove(final TaskId taskId) {\n        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            if (assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is still assigned to executors\");\n            }\n\n            if (!lockedTasks.contains(taskId)) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is not locked yet by the task manager\");\n            }\n\n            if (!tasks.containsInitialized(taskId)) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is not owned by the task manager\");\n            }\n\n            tasks.removeTask(tasks.initializedTask(taskId));\n        });\n\n        log.info(\"Removed task {} from the task manager\", taskId);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Set<ReadOnlyTask> getTasks() {\n        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(ReadOnlyTask::new).collect(Collectors.toSet()));\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setUncaughtException(final StreamsException exception, final TaskId taskId) {\n        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n\n            if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"An uncaught exception can only be set as long as the task is still assigned\");\n            }\n\n            if (uncaughtExceptions.containsKey(taskId)) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The uncaught exception must be cleared before restarting processing\");\n            }\n\n            uncaughtExceptions.put(taskId, exception);\n        });\n\n        log.info(\"Set an uncaught exception of type {} for task {}, with error message: {}\",\n            exception.getClass().getName(),\n            taskId,\n            exception.getMessage());\n    }\n\n    public Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> drainUncaughtExceptions() {\n        final Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> returnValue = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            final Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> result = new HashMap<>(uncaughtExceptions);\n            uncaughtExceptions.clear();\n            return result;\n        });\n\n        if (!returnValue.isEmpty()) {\n            log.debug(\"Drained {} uncaught exceptions\", returnValue.size());\n        }\n\n        return returnValue;\n    }\n\n    public boolean hasUncaughtException(final TaskId taskId) {\n        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> uncaughtExceptions.containsKey(taskId));\n    }\n\n    private void executeWithTasksLocked(final Runnable action) {\n        tasksLock.lock();\n        try {\n            action.run();\n        } finally {\n            tasksLock.unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private <T> T returnWithTasksLocked(final Supplier<T> action) {\n        tasksLock.lock();\n        try {\n            return action.get();\n        } finally {\n            tasksLock.unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private boolean canProgress(final StreamTask task, final long nowMs) {\n        return\n            taskExecutionMetadata.canProcessTask(task, nowMs) && task.isProcessable(nowMs) ||\n                taskExecutionMetadata.canPunctuateTask(task) && (task.canPunctuateStreamTime() || task.canPunctuateSystemTime());\n    }\n\n    public void startTaskExecutors() {\n        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n            t.start();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void shutdown(final Duration duration) {\n        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n            t.requestShutdown();\n        }\n        signalTaskExecutors();\n        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n            t.awaitShutdown(duration);\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":387,"startLine":1,"totalLines":387}}}

The `CachingKeyValueStore` has:\n- `lock` field: a `ReentrantReadWriteLock`\n- `position` field: a `Position` object used as a monitor (synchronized)\n\nAnd the `ThreadCache` has:\n- `this` monitor: from synchronized methods like `getOrCreateCache`, `getCache`, `resize`, `close`, `clear`\n- `NamedCache` monitors: from synchronized blocks `synchronized(cache)` in `put`, `flush`, etc.\n\n\n- `NamedCache` also has its own `this` monitor from synchronized methods like `get`, `put`, `evict`, `flush`\n\nNow I'm tracing through the lock acquisition order for each code path, starting with `CachingKeyValueStore.put()` from the stream thread, which acquires the write lock, then synchronizes on position, then enters ThreadCache and NamedCache monitors in sequence.\n\nFor `CachingKeyValueStore.query()` from the query thread, it acquires the read lock, synchronizes on position and wrapped position to get the current position, then enters the merged position monitor and accesses the cache through ThreadCache and NamedCache.\n\nFor `CachingKeyValueStore.get()` from non-stream threads, it's simpler—just the read lock followed by cache access through ThreadCache and NamedCache, with a fallback to the wrapped store on cache miss.\n\nFor `commit()` and `flushCache()`, the write lock is acquired first, then the cache flush operation begins...\n\nActually, looking at the next thinking, I see the flush path involves acquiring the ThreadCache monitor, releasing it, then acquiring the NamedCache monitor before flushing and applying listeners that ultimately write to the underlying store.\n\n`ThreadCache.resize()` is interesting because it can happen from any thread—it synchronizes on ThreadCache, then NamedCache, and triggers the same flush-and-forward chain to the underlying store.\n\nThe critical insight is that the eviction callback runs while holding the NamedCache monitor and writes directly to the underlying store, not back through the CachingKeyValueStore, so there's no circular locking between these two components. But I should check whether multiple CachingKeyValueStore instances could be sharing the same NamedCache and create a reverse lock ordering.\n\nWhen Store A calls `put()`, it acquires its own locks and eventually triggers eviction from its own NamedCache, but `maybeEvict()` only touches that specific cache, not others. However, `ThreadCache.resize()` is different—it synchronizes on the ThreadCache itself, then iterates through all NamedCache instances acquiring their monitors, and for each one it evicts and flushes through the listener callbacks. The resize operation doesn't try to acquire individual store locks while holding the ThreadCache lock, so that path also avoids the reverse ordering problem.\n\nNow I'm looking at the `getPosition()` method, which merges positions from both the store's own position and the wrapped store's position. The method synchronizes on both the local position and the wrapped position object, but I need to trace what `wrapped().getPosition()` actually returns—whether it's coming from something like a ChangeLoggingKeyValueBytesStore or MeteredKeyValueStore and what locks those might hold.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":334,"cache_read_input_tokens":73830,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":334,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"1d2b1ffa-0ec5-4400-b7bc-6a360e46f83a","uuid":"7e1643b9-d195-455b-b15d-06181261177b"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01YaUTfLTDUWWc2t8eNt466d","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Now I see the deadlock! Let me trace it carefully:\n\n`StoreQueryUtils.handleBasicQueries()` acquires `synchronized(position)` at line 128, and inside that synchronized block, it calls `runKeyQuery()` which at line 239 calls `keyValueStore.get(rawKeyQuery.getKey())`.\n\nIf `keyValueStore` is an `InMemoryKeyValueStore`, then `InMemoryKeyValueStore.get()` is `synchronized` on `this` (the InMemoryKeyValueStore instance).\n\nSo the lock order for `InMemoryKeyValueStore.query()` is:\n\n\nposition lock first, then the InMemoryKeyValueStore monitor.\n\nBut `InMemoryKeyValueStore.put()` does the opposite—it acquires the monitor first with `synchronized(this)`, then calls `putInternal()` which grabs the position lock. This creates a classic deadlock: `query()` locks position then monitor, while `put()` locks monitor then position. The reversed lock ordering between these two methods is exactly what causes the deadlock. - `query()` (via `handleBasicQueries`): InMemoryKVS.position → InMemoryKVS monitor (via `get()`)\n\nThread A: Calls `put()` → acquires `InMemoryKVS monitor` (synchronized put) → tries to acquire `InMemoryKVS.position` (in putInternal)\nThread B: Calls `query()` → acquires `InMemoryKVS.position` (in handleBasicQueries) → tries to acquire `InMemoryKVS monitor` (via get() inside runKeyQuery)\n\nDEADLOCK!\n\nI'm tracing through the code to confirm this. The `put()` method synchronizes on the instance, then `putInternal()` synchronizes on the position object, while `query()` does the opposite—it synchronizes on position first via `handleBasicQueries()`, then tries to acquire the instance lock when calling `get()`. This creates a classic ABBA deadlock where each thread holds one lock and waits for the other.\n\nThe fix is to enforce consistent lock ordering across both operations—either always acquire the instance lock first, then the position lock, or vice versa. The simplest approach is to remove the separate `synchronized(position)` block in `putInternal()` and rely on the existing synchronization strategy. I need to reconsider the lock ordering issue more carefully. The real problem is that `putInternal()` acquires `synchronized(position)` while already holding `synchronized(this)` from `put()`, but `query()` does the opposite—it acquires `synchronized(position)` first through `handleBasicQueries`, then tries to access the store. The cleanest solution might be to consolidate the locking strategy: either have `putInternal()` protect position updates using the store's own monitor instead of a separate position lock, or ensure `handleBasicQueries` doesn't independently lock position when it's being called from methods that already hold the store's lock.\n\nRewriting the method signature... InMemoryKeyValueStore implements KeyValueStore<Bytes, byte[]> {\n\n    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(InMemoryKeyValueStore.class);\n\n    private final String name;\n    private final NavigableMap<Bytes, byte[]> map = new TreeMap<>();\n    private final Position position = Position.emptyPosition();\n    private volatile boolean open = false;\n    private StateStoreContext context;\n\n    public InMemoryKeyValueStore(final String name) {\n        this.name = name;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String name() {\n        return name;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void init(final StateStoreContext stateStoreContext,\n                     final StateStore root) {\n        if (root != null) {\n            final boolean consistencyEnabled = StreamsConfig.InternalConfig.getBoolean(\n                stateStoreContext.appConfigs(),\n                IQ_CONSISTENCY_OFFSET_VECTOR_ENABLED,\n                false\n            );\n            // register the store\n            open = true;\n\n            stateStoreContext.register(\n                root,\n                (RecordBatchingStateRestoreCallback) records -> {\n                    synchronized (position) {\n                        for (final ConsumerRecord<byte[], byte[]> record : records) {\n                            put(Bytes.wrap(record.key()), record.value());\n                            ChangelogRecordDeserializationHelper.applyChecksAndUpdatePosition(\n                                record,\n                                consistencyEnabled,\n                                position\n                            );\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            );\n        }\n\n        open = true;\n        this.context = stateStoreContext;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean persistent() {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean isOpen() {\n        return open;\n    }\n\n
